"formulas hedge funds use" with FULL calculations and formulas, MAINLY for FOREX, GOLD, XAUUSD, and then all other markets

Institutional-Grade Order Flow, Liquidity, Impact and Indicator Specifications for FX and Gold


Data foundations and why FX and XAUUSD are special


A hard truth first (with love): the most “institutional” formulas in your list assume you have true trades (time & sales) and/or a central limit order book (Level 2, event messages). That assumption is often valid for exchange-traded futures, but is only partially true for spot FX and spot gold (XAUUSD) because they are largely over-the-counter (OTC) and fragmented across venues. citeturn11search1turn3search0turn3search3


In spot FX, trades occur via a mix of single-dealer streams, RFQ, and anonymous central limit order books (CLOBs) on some venues; there is no single consolidated “tape” or globally consolidated order book. citeturn3search0turn3search3turn11search1 In major centres, a high fraction of customer flow is internalised by dealers (matched “in-house”), which further reduces what any one venue feed can show you. citeturn11search5


For gold, spot trading is also largely OTC; entity["organization","London Bullion Market Association","bullion trade body, london uk"] describes entity["city","London","UK"] as the hub of the international OTC precious metals market and notes that “Good Delivery” bars settle over London accounts (“loco London”). citeturn12search0turn12search4 The entity["organization","World Gold Council","industry association"] similarly explains that the London OTC market has historically been central to gold trading and discusses liquidity across OTC and futures venues. citeturn12search8turn12search11


What institutions do in practice (and what you should mimic if you want “actual” order flow):


Use exchange venues (FX futures; gold futures) for “ground-truth” prints + depth and compute order-flow/LOB metrics there, because full-depth event data is obtainable. citeturn12search2turn12search6

Or use proprietary bank/ECN data (EBS/Reuters/LMAX/…); but any retail feed is, at best, a proxy.


The entity["organization","Bank for International Settlements","global financial institution"] Triennial Survey exists largely because decentralised OTC markets need statistical aggregation to describe size/structure; it is explicitly an OTC market survey collected from many dealers across jurisdictions. citeturn0search3turn0search7


Minimum data requirements mapping (so you don’t compute nonsense)


Signed trade flow / CVD / footprint delta / VPIN: Need trade prints (price, size, timestamp) and ideally aggressor side. If aggressor side is missing, you need a trade classification method. citeturn3search1turn9search1turn10search15

Book imbalance / OFI / absorption / resiliency: Need best bid/ask and sizes at least; ideally multiple book levels and event messages. citeturn4search2turn0search0turn14search17

VWAP/TWAP/POV/IS: Need at least prices; VWAP/POV also need volume (real volume ideally; otherwise tick volume proxy). citeturn2search7turn6search4turn6search3

Classical indicators (RSI/MACD/ADX/etc.): Only require OHLC (no microstructure). citeturn8search4turn8search1turn1search6


A key FX-specific caveat: high-frequency spot volume is often unavailable, so older FX research sometimes uses FX futures volume as a proxy, explicitly noting that lack of spot volume is a “principal handicap”. citeturn11search0


Signed trade flow and cumulative volume delta


Core definition: “order flow” as institutions mean it


In FX microstructure, order flow is commonly defined as a signed measure of trading volume: the net balance of buyer-initiated minus seller-initiated trades. citeturn11search3turn11search7


Let each trade be indexed by (i), with:


trade price (p_i)

trade size (volume) (q_i>0)

trade sign (\varepsilon_i \in {+1,-1}) where (+1) denotes buyer-initiated (aggressive buy), (-1) seller-initiated.


Then signed trade flow over an interval (I) is:
[
\mathrm{Flow}(I)=\sum_{i\in I} \varepsilon_i q_i
]
and CVD (cumulative volume delta) at time (t) is:
[
\mathrm{CVD}(t)=\sum_{i: t_i\le t} \varepsilon_i q_i
]
(Equivalent bar version: (\Delta V_k = V^{buy}_k - V^{sell}_k), then (\mathrm{CVD}k=\sum{j\le k}\Delta V_j).) citeturn9search1turn9search14


How to get (\varepsilon_i) when you don’t have aggressor side


If your feed does not label aggressor side, institutional practice is to estimate it using trade classification rules (imperfect, but standard).


Quote test (midquote rule):
Let midquote (m_i = (a_i + b_i)/2) from best ask (a_i), best bid (b_i).


If (p_i > m_i): classify buy ((\varepsilon_i=+1))

If (p_i < m_i): classify sell ((\varepsilon_i=-1))


Tick test (tick rule fallback):


(\varepsilon_i = \operatorname{sign}(p_i - p_{i-1})) when midquote test is inconclusive.


This family of rules is discussed and evaluated extensively in the microstructure literature; e.g. accuracy limits and biases are documented, and the Lee–Ready style approach uses quotes with tick fallback. citeturn3search1turn3search10turn3search7turn3search18


Institutional-grade warning: classification error is not random; it tends to worsen in certain conditions (eg inside-the-quote trades), so any downstream metric (CVD, VPIN, delta footprints) inherits those biases. citeturn3search18turn3search1


FX/XAUUSD reality check


For broker spot feeds, sometimes you only have quote updates (ticks) and maybe “tick volume”. That can still support a proxy CVD using price-change signs, but you must treat it as venue-specific pressure, not market-wide truth. The decentralised structure of FX is the reason this mismatch exists. citeturn11search1turn3search0turn11search0


Limit order book imbalance, event-based OFI, and resiliency


Book basics: spread and depth (the primitives)


In a limit order book, the bid–ask spread is (s_t = a_t - b_t), and depth refers to available resting quantity at price levels. These are core microstructure quantities in LOB research and practical trading. citeturn4search2turn14search17turn14search13


Book imbalance (OBI): a clean formula that is widely used


Define book levels (l=1,\dots,K) on each side with quantities (Q^b_{l,t}) (bid side) and (Q^a_{l,t}) (ask side). A common normalised order book imbalance is:
[
\mathrm{OBI}t(K)=\frac{\sum{l=1}^K Q^b_{l,t}-\sum_{l=1}^K Q^a_{l,t}}{\sum_{l=1}^K Q^b_{l,t}+\sum_{l=1}^K Q^a_{l,t}}
]
This produces a bounded measure in ([-1,1]): positive values indicate net bid-side dominance. citeturn4search2turn4search13


Practical “institutional” detail: choose (K) to match your venue’s meaningful depth. In some markets the top 1–5 levels dominate short-horizon moves; in others deeper liquidity matters. The LOB survey literature emphasises that measured effects depend on market design and data granularity. citeturn4search2


Event-based order flow imbalance (OFI): Cont’s specification


The “event-based” OFI formalises the idea that price changes over short horizons are mainly driven by imbalances in supply/demand at the best quotes. citeturn0search0turn0search8


Let the best bid price/size be ((P^b_t, Q^b_t)) and best ask be ((P^a_t, Q^a_t)). For each book update event (k) (event time), Cont et al. define contributions on bid and ask that handle both size changes at the same price and price changes that reset the queue. One standard representation is:


Bid contribution:
[
e^b_k=
\begin{cases}
Q^b_k, & P^b_k>P^b_{k-1}\
Q^b_k-Q^b_{k-1}, & P^b_k=P^b_{k-1}\
-,Q^b_{k-1}, & P^b_k<P^b_{k-1}
\end{cases}
]


Ask contribution:
[
e^a_k=
\begin{cases}
-,Q^a_{k-1}, & P^a_k>P^a_{k-1}\
Q^a_k-Q^a_{k-1}, & P^a_k=P^a_{k-1}\
Q^a_k, & P^a_k<P^a_{k-1}
\end{cases}
]


Then:
[
\mathrm{OFI}_k = e^b_k - e^a_k
]


Cont et al. report an approximately linear relation between mid-price changes and OFI, with sensitivity inversely related to depth. citeturn0search0turn0search8turn0search12


FX-specific constraint: you only get true OFI if your venue publishes L2 events. For many retail FX brokers, DOM is either absent, filtered, or not representative of the global market. That’s not your fault; it’s market structure. citeturn3search0turn11search1


Absorption, replenishment, and resiliency: how to make them measurable


“Absorption”, “replenishment”, and “resiliency” are often thrown around like vibes. You can make them rigorous by defining a liquidity shock and quantifying the book’s recovery.


A typical setup:


Define the midprice (m_t=(a_t+b_t)/2).

Define top-of-book depth (D_t = Q^b_t + Q^a_t) (or side-specific).

Define spread (s_t=a_t-b_t). citeturn14search17turn4search2turn4search8


Liquidity shock event: an “effective market order” (or aggressive sequence) that consumes liquidity and widens spread or reduces depth. This framing is used in resiliency studies. citeturn4search0turn4search8


Replenishment / resiliency as time-to-recovery
Let (t_0) be shock time; pick a recovery threshold (\alpha\in(0,1]) and baseline (D_{t_0^-}) (depth just before shock). Define time-to-depth-recovery:
[
T_D(\alpha)=\inf{\tau>0: D_{t_0+\tau}\ge \alpha D_{t_0^-}}
]
Similarly for spread recovery:
[
T_s(\beta)=\inf{\tau>0: s_{t_0+\tau}\le \beta s_{t_0^-}}
]
Resiliency in the microstructure sense is the replenishment process after shocks; modern papers explicitly connect it to order replenishment dynamics. citeturn4search8turn4search0turn4search16


Intensity-based replenishment
Many empirical designs measure how order intensity (limit order submissions/cancellations) changes around shocks, and how depth/spread evolve. citeturn4search0turn4search8


This is the correct “institutional” way to talk about absorption/replenishment: not mystical “they absorbed it”, but measurable book recovery properties.


Rolling impact coefficient, Kyle lambda, VPIN toxicity, and metaorder impact


Rolling impact coefficient: the operational definition


“Rolling impact coefficient” is typically an estimated slope linking price changes to an order-flow measure, updated through a rolling window (or exponential weighting). Cont et al.’s OFI model gives a canonical regression:
[
\Delta m_t = \beta \cdot \mathrm{OFI}_t + \epsilon_t
]
where (\Delta m_t) is the midprice change over the interval, (\mathrm{OFI}_t) is order flow imbalance, and (\beta) is the impact sensitivity. citeturn0search0turn0search12


A rolling estimate with window length (W) is:
[
\hat{\beta}t=\frac{\sum{j=t-W+1}^{t} (\mathrm{OFI}j-\overline{\mathrm{OFI}})(\Delta m_j-\overline{\Delta m})}{\sum{j=t-W+1}^{t} (\mathrm{OFI}_j-\overline{\mathrm{OFI}})^2}
]
(You can also use EWLS if you want faster adaptation.)


Institutional detail: because depth regimes change intraday, participants often normalise by depth or estimate state-dependent impact; the OFI paper explicitly discusses the role of depth in the slope. citeturn0search0turn4search2


Rolling impact as Kyle’s lambda (the classic)


In the entity["people","Albert Kyle","economist market microstructure"] model, price impact is often summarised by “Kyle’s (\lambda)”—the coefficient relating price change to signed order flow. The foundational paper is “Continuous Auctions and Insider Trading”. citeturn2search0


A common empirical estimate (discrete time) is:
[
\Delta p_t = \lambda \cdot \mathrm{Flow}_t + \eta_t
]
with (\mathrm{Flow}t=\sum{i\in t}\varepsilon_i q_i). Rolling (\hat{\lambda}_t) is computed exactly as the rolling (\hat{\beta}_t) above, substituting (\mathrm{Flow}). The interpretation is “price change per unit signed flow” (an illiquidity/impact proxy). citeturn2search0turn2search12turn2search20


Toxicity filter: VPIN (and why you must treat it carefully)


VPIN idea: replace clock time with a volume clock by slicing the tape into equal-volume buckets; compute volume imbalance per bucket; then VPIN is a moving average of these imbalances. citeturn0search13turn10search3turn10search10


Let bucket size be (V). For bucket (\tau):


(B_\tau) = estimated buy volume

(S_\tau) = estimated sell volume

(B_\tau + S_\tau = V)


Bucket imbalance:
[
I_\tau=\frac{|B_\tau-S_\tau|}{V}
]


VPIN over last (n) buckets:
[
\mathrm{VPIN}t=\frac{1}{n}\sum{\tau=t-n+1}^{t} I_\tau
]


The literature explains VPIN as an order-flow toxicity gauge derived from the PIN lineage. citeturn0search13turn10search3turn10search6


How to estimate (B_\tau) and (S_\tau): BVC-style bulk classification


If you don’t have aggressor-labelled trades, a standard approach is bulk volume classification (BVC): estimate the fraction of volume that is buyer-initiated based on bar return relative to volatility (often using a Normal CDF). Modern papers discuss BVC as a probabilistic method to assign direction to pre-aggregated volume. citeturn10search1turn10search11turn10search15


A widely used specification is:
[
\hat{B}\tau = V\tau \cdot \Phi!\left(\frac{r_\tau}{\hat{\sigma}}\right), \qquad \hat{S}\tau = V\tau - \hat{B}\tau
]
where (r\tau) is the bucket return and (\hat{\sigma}) is a return scale (often rolling). (Exact implementation details vary, but the “probabilistic buy fraction via CDF” is the defining feature.) citeturn10search1turn10search15turn9search2


The “institutional” warning label (non-negotiable)


VPIN is contested. A prominent critique argues the metric can be mechanically correlated with volume and volatility innovations depending on implementation, questioning predictive utility for stress timing. citeturn3search2turn3search11 There is also a published rejoinder defending VPIN and disputing aspects of the critique. citeturn3search5turn10search3


So, if you want a real toxicity filter you can defend in a professional setting, you treat VPIN as:


one feature among others (impact slope, spread blowouts, depth decay, volatility jump, etc.), not “the” answer; and

extremely sensitive to classification method, bucket size, window length, and market regime. citeturn3search2turn10search1turn10search17


Metaorder impact for execution: square-root law and lifecycle


A “metaorder” is a large parent order executed incrementally (split into child orders). This is the standard framing in market impact theory and execution research. citeturn2search5turn2search2


A widely documented empirical regularity is concave impact, often approximated by a square-root relationship between peak impact and participation rate. Farmer et al. derive a theory consistent with square-root behaviour and discuss temporary vs permanent components. citeturn2search5turn2search1


A common “execution desk” parameterisation:


Let sign (s\in{+1,-1})

Daily volatility (\sigma_d)

Metaorder size (Q)

Daily volume (V_d)

Participation (\phi = Q/V_d)


Then peak (temporary) impact:
[
I_{\text{temp}} \approx s \cdot Y \cdot \sigma_d \sqrt{\phi}
]
where (Y) is an empirical coefficient ((\mathcal{O}(1)), market-dependent). citeturn2search5turn2search2


Bacry et al. study market impact across multiple time scales and discuss the “life cycle” of investor orders (including post-trade decay/relaxation dynamics). citeturn2search2turn2search6


FX/XAUUSD caveat: to use this properly you need a defensible (V_d) and (\sigma_d). For spot FX and broker XAUUSD you often approximate (V_d) with either (a) futures volume proxy, or (b) venue-specific tick volume; both come with interpretation risk. citeturn11search0turn11search1turn11search8


VWAP, TWAP, POV, implementation shortfall, and volatility inputs


This section is where execution becomes maths rather than folklore.


VWAP: benchmark and live estimator


The canonical VWAP over a session is:
[
\mathrm{VWAP}=\frac{\sum_{i} p_i q_i}{\sum_{i} q_i}
]
A common bar approximation uses “typical price” (TP_t=(H_t+L_t+C_t)/3):
[
\mathrm{VWAP}\approx \frac{\sum_t TP_t \cdot V_t}{\sum_t V_t}
]
These definitions are widely documented in trading references. citeturn2search7


Institutional nuance: for FX and CFDs, “volume” may be tick volume, not traded volume—so your VWAP is then a tick-weighted average, not a true VWAP. That can still be useful as a consistent internal benchmark, but it’s not the same object as futures VWAP. citeturn11search0turn11search1


TWAP: schedule benchmark


TWAP over (N) evenly spaced time points is:
[
\mathrm{TWAP}=\frac{1}{N}\sum_{k=1}^{N} p_{t_k}
]
Execution-wise, TWAP typically implies sending child orders according to a time schedule rather than volume. TWAP is a standard schedule-based algorithm class. citeturn6search17


POV: participation rate execution


POV targets a participation rate (\rho\in(0,1)) of observed market volume. IBKR’s documentation describes POV rate as the percentage of traded volume the algo targets. citeturn6search3turn6search19


Discrete interval version (interval (k)):


observed market volume (V^{mkt}_k)

desired child size (x_k=\rho , V^{mkt}_k)

execute (q_k=\min(x_k, Q_{\text{remaining}}))


This is the “actual” mechanical definition behind the marketing. citeturn6search3turn2search3


Implementation Shortfall (IS): the institutional gold-standard cost metric


Perold’s implementation shortfall concept measures the gap between the paper portfolio (instant fill at decision price) and the realised portfolio after execution; a survey paper on IS algorithms cites this definition and frames IS as a benchmark for execution quality. citeturn6search4turn6search17


For a buy order of target size (Q):


decision (arrival) price (P_0)

executed fills ((p_i, q_i)) for (i=1..n) with (\sum q_i = Q_{\text{filled}})

final benchmark price at horizon end (P_T) for unfilled shares

commissions/fees (C)


A common dollar shortfall expression:
[
\mathrm{IS} = \left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} p_i q_i\right) + (Q-Q_{\text{filled}})P_T - QP_0 + C
]
This embeds both execution cost and opportunity cost (unfilled quantity). citeturn6search4turn6search12


Almgren–Chriss: “actual” optimal execution maths


The entity["people","Robert Almgren","quant researcher optimal execution"] – entity["people","Neil Chriss","portfolio execution researcher"] framework explicitly optimises the trade-off between market impact costs and risk from price volatility during execution, producing an efficient frontier for liquidation strategies. citeturn0search6


In its classic discrete-time form, you choose a trading schedule (x_k) to minimise:
[
\mathbb{E}[C(x)] + \gamma , \mathrm{Var}(C(x))
]
where (C(x)) includes temporary and permanent impact components and (\gamma) is risk aversion. citeturn0search6turn6search21


This model is the backbone behind many institutional scheduling engines (even when extended, nonlinear, stochastic, or constrained). citeturn0search6turn6search17


Volatility inputs: realised volatility and range estimators


Execution and impact models need a volatility estimate (\sigma). In market microstructure/econometrics, a standard realised variance estimator is the sum of squared high-frequency returns; realised volatility is its square root. citeturn5search8turn5search12


If you only have OHLC (common in FX spot), range-based estimators (Parkinson, Garman–Klass, Rogers–Satchell, etc.) are standard alternatives and are discussed in volatility estimation references (including exchange education notes). citeturn5search13turn5search17turn5search18


Market Profile, Volume Profile, Footprint, and VSA as measurable objects


Market Profile and TPO: time-at-price as a histogram


Market Profile is based on Time Price Opportunity (TPO) and is attributed to entity["people","J. Peter Steidlmayer","cbot trader market profile"] at the entity["organization","Chicago Board of Trade","futures exchange, chicago"]; modern platform documentation describes TPO profiles as blocks of time spent at price levels. citeturn1search12turn1search4


A rigorous construction:


Partition a session into time brackets (b=1..B) (eg 30-minute periods).

Define price bins (j=1..J).

Let (I_{j,b}=1) if price traded in bin (j) during bracket (b), else (0).


Then:
[
\mathrm{TPO}j = \sum{b=1}^{B} I_{j,b}
]
Point of Control (POC):
[
\mathrm{POC}=\arg\max_j \mathrm{TPO}_j
]
Value Area is typically defined as a contiguous set of bins around POC that contains a chosen percentage of activity; many tools use 70% by default (mirroring “value area” conventions). citeturn9search0


Volume Profile: volume-at-price and value area calculation


Volume Profile is volume aggregated by price bins over a selected range. Platform documentation provides an explicit algorithm to compute value area by starting at POC (highest-volume row) and expanding up/down until reaching the target percentage (often 70%). citeturn9search0


Formally, if (V_j) is volume in price bin (j):


( \mathrm{POC} = \arg\max_j V_j)

Find the minimal contiguous set (S) containing POC such that:
[
\frac{\sum_{j\in S} V_j}{\sum_{j=1}^{J} V_j}\ge \theta
]
with (\theta=0.70) default in many implementations. citeturn9search0


Footprint (bid/ask volume at price) and delta


Footprint charts display buyer vs seller volume at each price level inside the candle; documentation describes “Buy and Sell” columns and a “Delta” option showing the difference between buyer and seller volume per level. citeturn9search1


Let (V^{ask}{k,j}) be volume executed at price bin (j) in bar (k) by aggressive buys (lifting offers), and (V^{bid}{k,j}) aggressive sells (hitting bids). Then:
[
\Delta_{k,j} = V^{ask}{k,j} - V^{bid}{k,j}
]
Bar delta:
[
\Delta_k = \sum_{j=1}^{J} \Delta_{k,j}
]
CVD is then cumulative (\sum \Delta_k). citeturn9search1turn9search14


image_group{"layout":"carousel","aspect_ratio":"16:9","query":["volume footprint chart bid ask delta example","volume profile POC value area example","market profile TPO example chart","order book heatmap liquidity example"],"num_per_query":1}


Volume Spread Analysis (VSA): formalise the inputs, not the mythology


VSA (popularised by entity["people","Tom Williams","volume spread analysis author"]) frames interpretation around three bar attributes: volume, price spread (range), and close position. citeturn9search8turn9search11


Even if pattern labels vary, you can compute the core measurable features:


Spread (range):
[
\mathrm{Spread}_t = H_t - L_t
]

Close location value (CLV), a common “who won the bar” proxy:
[
\mathrm{CLV}_t = \frac{(C_t - L_t) - (H_t - C_t)}{H_t - L_t}
= \frac{2C_t - H_t - L_t}{H_t - L_t}
]

Volume normalisation (z-score over window (n)):
[
zV_t = \frac{V_t - \mu(V_{t-n+1:t})}{\sigma(V_{t-n+1:t})}
]


Those transforms give you a defensible, programmable VSA feature set—particularly important in FX where volume is often tick volume and must be treated consistently. citeturn11search0turn11search1turn9search8


Liquidity events, dark pools, structural “zones”, and classical indicators


Liquidity: measurable quantities you can actually price


The realistic institutional toolkit for “liquidity” is built on transaction cost and impact, not vibes.


For a trade at time (t) of price (p_t), with midquote (m_t) and sign (\varepsilon_t):


Effective spread (cost of immediacy):
[
\mathrm{ES}_t = 2;|p_t - m_t|
]


Price impact over horizon (\Delta):
[
\mathrm{PI}t(\Delta)= 2,\varepsilon_t,(m{t+\Delta}-m_t)
]


Realised spread (net of adverse selection/impact):
[
\mathrm{RS}_t(\Delta)=\mathrm{ES}_t - \mathrm{PI}_t(\Delta)
]


These decompositions are standard in transaction cost analysis discussions. citeturn4search11turn1search1


Stops, cascades, “hunts”, “grabs”, and sweeps: what research supports


“Stop hunt” is a trader label. What research does support is that stop-loss orders can contribute to self-reinforcing price cascades in FX. entity["people","Carol L. Osler","economist fx microstructure"] provides evidence and explicitly discusses rapid price movements catalysed by stop-loss orders. citeturn14search4turn14search2


A defensible quantitative specification for “stop-run / cascade candidate” (data permitting) is a composite of:


threshold breach: price exceeds recent swing high/low by (\delta)

speed: realised volatility or return over short horizon spikes

liquidity shock signature: depth drops and/or spread widens, then recovers (resiliency)

reversal probability: price returns into the prior range within (T)


Those components are consistent with microstructure shock/resiliency concepts, even if your discretionary label differs. citeturn4search8turn4search0turn14search4


Sweeps (book sweeps): in LOB terms, a sweep is an aggressive order that consumes liquidity across multiple price levels. That’s exactly how market orders interact with book depth: they match against successive levels until filled. citeturn14search20turn14search17
A simple sweep index (requires L2/trade prints):


Let (L_t) be number of distinct price levels consumed by aggressive flow in a short interval (t).

Define sweep flag ( \mathbf{1}[L_t \ge 2]) and sweep intensity (L_t) or consumed notional.

In US equities context, the entity["organization","Securities and Exchange Commission","us securities regulator"] defines an Alternative Trading System (ATS) as a trading system meeting the definition of an exchange but operating under Regulation ATS exemptions; ATS lists and definitions are public. citeturn7search1 Dark pools are commonly described as a type of ATS designed for less-displayed liquidity. citeturn7search5turn7search13

Supply & demand zones, order blocks, Wyckoff phases, and FVGs: make them explicit


A lot of these concepts are not standardised academically. If you want “institutional” behaviour, the key is: define them unambiguously so they can be tested.


Wyckoff market phases


Wyckoff schematics are widely taught in technical analysis communities; Hank Pruden’s schematic write-up outlines accumulation/distribution phases and their interpretation within the Wyckoff method structure. citeturn7search0turn7search4


A testable way to encode “phase” is typically based on:


range detection (volatility compression)

breakout and retest behaviour

volume/effort vs result proxies (where volume is reliable)


(Just be honest: the phase labels are a model; the data doesn’t come with “Accumulation” stamped on it.) citeturn7search0turn9search0


Order blocks (SMC-style)


Common trading-education definitions describe a bullish order block as the last bearish candle before an impulsive bullish move (and vice versa). citeturn7search3turn7search7
A strict, programmable definition might be:


detect an impulse move with return (|r|\ge k \cdot \mathrm{ATR})

define the “order block candle” as the last opposite-colour candle immediately prior

zone = ([L_{ob}, H_{ob}]) (or body-only)


This does not prove “institutions placed orders there”; it defines a repeatable price-structure zone consistent with that narrative.


Fair Value Gaps (FVG)


Many practitioner sources define FVG as a three-candle imbalance; for bullish case a common rule is: high of candle 1 < low of candle 3, leaving a gap region within candle 2’s range. citeturn7search2turn7search14turn7search10


A strict bullish FVG definition:


For candles (t-2,t-1,t):
[
\text{Bullish FVG exists if } H_{t-2} < L_{t}
]
Gap zone:
[
\text{FVG zone} = [H_{t-2}, L_{t}]
]
Bearish analogue:
[
L_{t-2} > H_{t}
\quad\Rightarrow\quad
\text{zone} = [H_{t}, L_{t-2}]
]
Again: definable and testable, regardless of the story you tell around it.


Classical indicators and level mathematics (full formulas)


RSI


RSI is:
[
\mathrm{RSI}=100-\frac{100}{1+\mathrm{RS}}
\quad\text{where}\quad
\mathrm{RS}=\frac{\text{AvgGain}_n}{\text{AvgLoss}_n}
]
Many platforms use Wilder’s smoothing (RMA) after the initial average. citeturn8search4turn8search0


MACD


[
\mathrm{MACD} = \mathrm{EMA}{12}(C) - \mathrm{EMA}{26}(C)
]
Signal line:
[
\mathrm{Signal}=\mathrm{EMA}_{9}(\mathrm{MACD})
]
Histogram:
[
\mathrm{Hist}=\mathrm{MACD}-\mathrm{Signal}
]
This is the standard MACD construction. citeturn8search5turn8search1


Stochastic RSI


StochRSI is typically:
[
\mathrm{StochRSI}=\frac{\mathrm{RSI}t - \min(\mathrm{RSI}{t-n+1:t})}{\max(\mathrm{RSI}{t-n+1:t})-\min(\mathrm{RSI}{t-n+1:t})}
]
Often with smoothing to produce %K/%D style lines. citeturn1search19turn1search3


ROC


Rate of Change over (n) periods:
[
\mathrm{ROC}= \frac{C_t - C_{t-n}}{C_{t-n}}\times 100
]
This is the standard definition used in indicator guides. citeturn13search12turn13search0


ADX (Directional Movement System)


ADX is derived from True Range (TR), directional movement (+DM, −DM), smoothed directional indicators (+DI, −DI), then DX and ADX smoothing. Public references summarise the full chain and formula structure. citeturn1search6turn1search2turn1search10


A compact canonical form:


(\mathrm{ADX}=100 \cdot \mathrm{EMA}\Big(\frac{|(+DI)-(-DI)|}{(+DI)+(-DI)}\Big)) (with Wilder-style smoothing in many implementations). citeturn1search6


Correlation coefficient (Pearson)


For two return series (x_t, y_t) over window (n):
[
r=\frac{\sum_{t=1}^{n}(x_t-\bar{x})(y_t-\bar{y})}{\sqrt{\sum_{t=1}^{n}(x_t-\bar{x})^2}\sqrt{\sum_{t=1}^{n}(y_t-\bar{y})^2}}
]
This is standard Pearson correlation. citeturn13search3turn13search7


Pivot levels (floor pivots)


Classic pivot point:
[
P=\frac{H+L+C}{3}
]
First resistance/support:
[
R_1=2P-L,\qquad S_1=2P-H
]
Second resistance/support:
[
R_2=P+(H-L),\qquad S_2=P-(H-L)
]
These formulas are widely published. citeturn8search7turn8search15


Support/resistance and trendlines


These are not single formulas; they are constructions (often pivot-based). Mainstream definitions describe trendlines as lines connecting pivot highs or lows to indicate direction and potential support/resistance. citeturn13search2turn13search14


A simple parametric trendline through two pivots ((t_1,P_1)), ((t_2,P_2)):
[
\text{slope } m=\frac{P_2-P_1}{t_2-t_1},\qquad \hat{P}(t)=P_1+m(t-t_1)
]
(Use time index in bars; the concept is geometric.)


Fibonacci retracement levels


Given a swing low (L) and swing high (H), retracement level at ratio (r) (e.g., 0.236, 0.382, 0.5, 0.618, 0.786) for an up-move is:
[
\mathrm{Level}(r)=H - r(H-L)
]
For a down-move, symmetry gives:
[
\mathrm{Level}(r)=L + r(H-L)
]
The commonly used ratios are widely listed in trading references. citeturn13search1turn13search9

Institutional-spec version — the one worth coding


There is no single public “hedge fund formula sheet”. Real shops run venue-specific, asset-specific stacks. But there is a public canonical core: market microstructure models for order flow, impact, toxicity, liquidity, and execution, plus a separate layer of technical/state features. For spot FX and XAUUSD, the big constraint is structural: these are largely OTC and fragmented, so there is no single consolidated tape or single consolidated order book like centralized futures/equities. BIS describes spot FX as decentralized, venue-fragmented, executed across dealer systems, CLOBs, RFQ venues, and single-bank platforms; in recent data, dealer internalisation has been very high in major hubs. LBMA’s published trade-reporting data shows very large OTC gold turnover, but still not a retail-style full public depth feed. For U.S. dark pools/ATSs, FINRA publishes only delayed transparency data, not real-time institutional flow. finra.org+4Bank for International Settlements+4Bank for International Settlements+4


So the right way to build this for FOREX / XAUUSD first is:


Use canonical institutional microstructure models where the market supports them directly.

Use venue-specific or proxy versions where OTC fragmentation blocks direct observation.

Treat price-action concepts like order blocks, Wyckoff phases, FVGs, liquidity sweeps, supply/demand as formalized state variables, not as if they were exchange-native primitives.

Fuse everything into one normalized state vector for alpha, execution, and risk. That is the actual professional architecture. FX microstructure research explicitly centers order flow, heterogeneity, private information, and liquidity as the key short-run drivers. Norges Bank+1



Base market variables



Let:


$$
b_t = \text{best bid},\quad a_t=\text{best ask},\quad s_t=a_t-b_t,\quad m_t=\frac{a_t+b_t}{2}
$$


Top-of-book sizes:


$$
q^b_t=\text{best bid size},\qquad q^a_t=\text{best ask size}
$$


Microprice:


$$
\mu_t=\frac{a_t,q^b_t+b_t,q^a_t}{q^b_t+q^a_t}
$$


Relative spread:


$$
s_t^{rel}=\frac{a_t-b_t}{m_t}
$$


For centralized markets, these are direct. For spot FX/XAUUSD, compute them per venue and then aggregate across venues  $v$  with reliability/liquidity weights  $w_v$ :


$$
m_t^{agg}=\sum_v w_v m_{t,v},\qquad s_t^{agg}=\sum_v w_v s_{t,v},\qquad \sum_v w_v=1
$$


That venue-weighted approach is mandatory in OTC/fragmented markets because the venue mix itself carries information. Bank for International Settlements+2Bank for International Settlements+2



Signed trade flow / CVD



For each trade  $i$ , define aggressor sign:


$$
\epsilon_i \in {-1,+1}
$$


and signed volume:


$$
f_i=\epsilon_i v_i
$$


Then:


$$
\text{SignedFlow}{[t-h,t]}=\sum{i \in [t-h,t]} \epsilon_i v_i
$$


$$
\text{CVD}t=\text{CVD}{t-1}+\sum_{i \in (t-\Delta,t]} \epsilon_i v_i
$$


If trade direction is not given, infer it using quote/tick classification; this is standard in microstructure work, though no classifier is perfect. For OTC FX/XAUUSD, use the best available aggressor tags from your venue or prime broker; otherwise fall back to tick-direction, quote-move inference, or dealer-side flow flags. The point is simple: CVD is only as good as the trade-sign classification feeding it. EconPapers+2ScienceDirect+2



Book imbalance (OBI)



Top-of-book imbalance:


$$
OBI^{(1)}_t=\frac{q^b_t-q^a_t}{q^b_t+q^a_t}
$$


Multi-level imbalance over  $K$  levels:


$$
OBI^{(K)}t= \frac{\sum{k=1}^{K} w_k B_{k,t}-\sum_{k=1}^{K} w_k A_{k,t}} {\sum_{k=1}^{K} w_k B_{k,t}+\sum_{k=1}^{K} w_k A_{k,t}}
$$


where  $B_{k,t}$  and  $A_{k,t}$  are displayed bid/ask depth at level  $k$ , and  $w_k$  usually decays with distance from the top of book, e.g.


$$
w_k=e^{-\alpha(k-1)}
$$


For FX/XAUUSD, never pretend you have “the” order book unless you actually do. You only have a venue-local book or an aggregator book. So build:


$$
OBI^{agg}t=\sum_v w_v,OBI{t,v}^{(K)}
$$


and attach a coverage confidence score based on how much of tradable liquidity your data actually sees. The professional mistake is not bad math; it is false observability. Bank for International Settlements+2Bank for International Settlements+2



Event-based OFI (the actual institutional microstructure primitive)



Using the Cont–Kukanov–Stoikov event definition, with  $P_n^B,P_n^A$  the best bid/ask prices and  $q_n^B,q_n^A$  their sizes:


$$
e_n = \mathbf{1}{{P_n^B \ge P{n-1}^B}}q_n^B -\mathbf{1}{{P_n^B \le P{n-1}^B}}q_{n-1}^B -\mathbf{1}{{P_n^A \le P{n-1}^A}}q_n^A +\mathbf{1}{{P_n^A \ge P{n-1}^A}}q_{n-1}^A
$$


Aggregate over a window:


$$
OFI_t=\sum_{n \in W_t} e_n
$$


Then the short-horizon price-impact model is:


$$
\Delta m_t = \beta_t,OFI_t+\varepsilon_t
$$


Institutional relevance: this is not retail folklore. Cont, Kukanov, and Stoikov show that short-horizon price changes are driven mainly by OFI, with a slope approximately inverse to market depth. That is exactly why OFI belongs in the core state vector. EconPapers+2OUP Academic+2



Rolling impact coefficient



You want two rolling impact coefficients, not one.


5.1 OFI impact coefficient


$$
\beta_t = \arg\min_\beta \sum_{u=t-L+1}^{t} \omega_u\left(\Delta m_u-\beta,OFI_u\right)^2
$$


5.2 Signed-flow / Kyle–Hasbrouck style coefficient


$$
\lambda_t = \arg\min_\lambda \sum_{u=t-L+1}^{t} \omega_u\left(\Delta m_u-\lambda,Q_u^{sign}\right)^2
$$


with:


$$
Q_u^{sign}=\sum_{i \in u}\epsilon_i v_i
$$


or a concave-size version:


$$
\Delta m_u=\lambda_t ,\text{sign}(Q_u),|Q_u|^\psi+\eta_u,\qquad 0<\psi\le 1
$$


Interpretation:  $\beta_t$  tells you how sensitive the book is to queue imbalance;  $\lambda_t$  tells you how sensitive the price is to signed executed flow. Hasbrouck’s framework is about the ultimate price impact of trade innovations; Kyle’s  $\lambda$  is the classic liquidity/impact parameter. In live systems, both should be estimated rolling, robustly, and by session. EconPapers+2Econometric Society+2



Toxicity filter (VPIN-style)



Set a fixed bucket size  $V^*$ . For each volume bucket  $b$ :


$$
V_b^+ = \text{buy volume in bucket } b,\qquad V_b^- = \text{sell volume in bucket } b
$$


Volume imbalance per bucket:


$$
I_b=\frac{|V_b^+ - V_b^-|}{V^*}
$$


VPIN over the last  $N$  buckets:


$$
VPIN_t=\frac{1}{N}\sum_{b=t-N+1}^{t} I_b
$$


This is a toxicity / adverse-selection feature, not an oracle. Easley, López de Prado, and O’Hara propose VPIN as a volume-time toxicity measure. Later work challenged its robustness and argued that some predictive power is mechanical or classification-driven. The sane institutional use is therefore: keep VPIN-style toxicity as one filter among many, and always pair it with better trade classification and explicit spread/depth/resiliency checks. pure.au.dk+3IDEAS/RePEc+3OUP Academic+3



Absorption / replenishment / resiliency



This is where people usually get sloppy. Don’t.


7.1 Absorption


Measure how much aggressive volume is absorbed with little price displacement:


$$
Abs_t^{+}=\frac{V^{aggr,+}{W_t}}{1+\left|\Delta m{W_t}\right|/\delta p} ,\qquad Abs_t^{-}=\frac{V^{aggr,-}{W_t}}{1+\left|\Delta m{W_t}\right|/\delta p}
$$


Large same-side aggressive volume with small move = strong absorption.


7.2 Replenishment


After a liquidity shock, measure how much displayed depth refills:


$$
Repl_t^{b}=\frac{D^{b}{post}-D^{b}{min}}{V^{sell}{shock}+\epsilon} ,\qquad Repl_t^{a}=\frac{D^{a}{post}-D^{a}{min}}{V^{buy}{shock}+\epsilon}
$$


7.3 Resiliency


Let  $\tau_s$  be the time for spread to revert to baseline and  $\tau_d$  the time for depth to revert. Then a compact resiliency score is:


$$
Resil_t=\exp!\left(-\frac{\tau_s}{\bar\tau_s}\right)\exp!\left(-\frac{\tau_d}{\bar\tau_d}\right)
$$


or, if you estimate impulse responses directly, use half-lives from the spread/depth/intensity response. The literature on order-book resiliency is explicit: liquidity shocks widen/spoil liquidity immediately, then replenishment speed matters enormously. ScienceDirect+1



Metaorder impact for execution



For a parent order of size  $Q$ , daily volume  $V_D$ , and volatility  $\sigma_D$ , the public institutional benchmark is the square-root impact law:


$$
I(Q)\approx Y,\sigma_D \sqrt{\frac{Q}{V_D}}
$$


A participation-aware version is:


$$
I(Q,\gamma)\approx Y,\sigma_D \sqrt{\frac{Q}{V_D}},f(\gamma)
$$


where  $\gamma$  is participation rate. Recent work continues to support square-root scaling at the metaorder level, with participation effects layered on top. This is execution math, not social-media incense. Taylor & Francis Online+2EconPapers+2


For explicit scheduling, use an Almgren–Chriss-style objective:


$$
\min_{{n_k}} \mathbb{E}[C]+\Lambda,\mathrm{Var}(C)
$$


subject to:


$$
X_k=X_{k-1}-n_k,\qquad X_0=Q,\qquad X_N=0
$$


with price dynamics:


$$
S_k=S_{k-1}+\sigma \sqrt{\tau},\xi_k+\gamma n_k
$$


execution price:


$$
\tilde S_k=S_k+\eta \frac{n_k}{\tau}
$$


and cost:


$$
C=\sum_{k=1}^{N} n_k \tilde S_k - Q S_0
$$


That gives you the implementation-shortfall backbone. Econometric Society+2Mathematical Institute, Oxford+2



VWAP, TWAP, POV



VWAP benchmark


$$
VWAP_{[0,T]}=\frac{\sum_{i} p_i v_i}{\sum_i v_i}
$$


TWAP benchmark


$$
TWAP_{[0,T]}=\frac{1}{N}\sum_{k=1}^{N} p_k
$$


or in continuous time:


$$
TWAP=\frac{1}{T}\int_0^T p(t),dt
$$


POV schedule


With participation rate  $\rho$ :


$$
n_t=\min\left(X_{rem,t}, \rho,V^{mkt}_t\right)
$$


Schedule targets


If  $u(t)$  is the expected intraday volume curve:


$$
\int_0^T u(t),dt=1,\qquad x'(t)=Q,u(t)
$$


Kato shows VWAP is optimal for a risk-neutral trader in an Almgren–Chriss setting with a volume process; TWAP is the flat-time benchmark; POV is the “stay a fixed fraction of market flow” schedule. In practice, serious execution stacks switch among these depending on urgency, toxicity, spread, and liquidity refill. J-STAGE+2Mathematical Institute, Oxford+2



Volatility layer



Use four estimators, not one.


10.1 Realized variance


$$
RV_t=\sum_{i=1}^{M} r_{t,i}^2
$$


10.2 Parkinson range estimator


$$
\sigma^2_{P,t}= \frac{1}{4\ln 2}\cdot \frac{1}{n}\sum_{j=1}^{n}\left(\ln\frac{H_j}{L_j}\right)^2
$$


10.3 Garman–Klass


$$
\sigma^2_{GK,t} = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{j=1}^{n} \left[ \frac{1}{2}\left(\ln\frac{H_j}{L_j}\right)^2 -(2\ln 2-1)\left(\ln\frac{C_j}{O_j}\right)^2 \right]
$$


10.4 Bipower variation


$$
BV_t=\mu_1^{-2}\sum_{i=2}^{M}|r_i||r_{i-1}|, \qquad \mu_1=\sqrt{\frac{2}{\pi}}
$$


Use  $RV-BV$  as a jump proxy. Range-based estimators are often more efficient than simple close-to-close measures, while bipower variation is useful for separating continuous variation from jumps. Cambridge University Press & Assessment+3EconPapers+3revstat.ine.pt+3



VSA, footprint, volume profile, market profile



These are not exchange-native primitives in OTC FX, so formalize them.


11.1 VSA quant layer


For bar  $t$ :


$$
Spread_t = H_t-L_t
$$


$$
CLV_t=\frac{2C_t-H_t-L_t}{H_t-L_t+\epsilon}
$$


$$
VolZ_t=\frac{V_t-\mu_V}{\sigma_V}
$$


$$
ER_t = \frac{C_t-O_t}{ATR_t+\epsilon}\cdot VolZ_t
$$


Then define pattern scores such as:


No demand: up bar, narrow spread, low  $VolZ$ , weak  $CLV$

Stopping volume: down bar, extreme  $VolZ$ , strong rebound  $CLV$

Effort vs result: high  $|VolZ|$  with low normalized range or low net progress


That is the quant way to make VSA testable.


11.2 Footprint


At price level  $p$  within bar  $t$ :


$$
\Delta_{p,t}=V^+{p,t}-V^-{p,t}
$$


$$
\Delta_t=\sum_p \Delta_{p,t}
$$


Ask-side imbalance:


$$
Imb^+{p,t}=\frac{V^+{p,t}}{V^-_{p-\delta p,t}+\epsilon}
$$


Bid-side imbalance:


$$
Imb^-{p,t}=\frac{V^-{p,t}}{V^+_{p+\delta p,t}+\epsilon}
$$


Stacked imbalance =  $k$  consecutive price levels where imbalance ratio exceeds threshold  $\theta$ .


11.3 Volume profile


For price bins  $j$ :


$$
VP_j=\sum_{i:,p_i \in bin_j} v_i
$$


$$
POC=\arg\max_j VP_j
$$


Construct Value Area by expanding outward from  $POC$  until cumulative volume reaches target fraction, usually 70%.


11.4 Market profile (TPO)


$$
TPO_j=\sum_{\tau \in session}\mathbf{1}{{price\tau \in bin_j}}
$$


Volume Profile gives where volume traded; Market Profile gives where time was accepted. Those are related, not identical. Volumetrica Trading+2Overcharts+2



Liquidity: hunts, grabs, sweeps, slippage, spread, depth



A robust institutional-style liquidity module for price-action concepts:


12.1 Liquidity pool


Let  $SH$  be a prior swing high cluster and  $SL$  a swing low cluster. A price level becomes a liquidity pool if repeated pivots lie within tolerance band  $\epsilon_L$ .


12.2 Sweep / stop run


Bullish sweep of lows:


$$
L_t < SL - \epsilon_L \quad\text{and}\quad C_t > SL
$$


Bearish sweep of highs:


$$
H_t > SH + \epsilon_L \quad\text{and}\quad C_t < SH
$$


Excursion score:


$$
SweepScore_t= w_1\frac{\text{excursion beyond pool}}{ATR_t} +w_2\frac{\text{reversal distance}}{ATR_t} +w_3|\Delta CVD_t| +w_4|OFI_t|
$$


12.3 Spread shock


$$
SpreadShock_t=\frac{s_t-\mu_s}{\sigma_s}
$$


12.4 Slippage


$$
Slippage = \text{signed}(side)\cdot \frac{P_{exec}-P_{decision}}{tick}
$$


12.5 Depth fade


$$
DepthFade_t = 1 - \frac{D_t}{\mu_D}
$$


For FX/XAUUSD, spread changes, quote fade, slippage, and venue-to-venue dislocations are often more observable than “real” consolidated stop-flow. So code what the market actually gives you, not what YouTube promised you. Bank for International Settlements+2Bank for International Settlements+2



Dark pools



For U.S. equities, dark/off-exchange flow can be proxied with FINRA ATS/OTC data, but it is delayed, not a real-time execution edge by itself. For spot FX, “dark pool” is the wrong mental model much of the time; the better framework is internalisation, dealer inventory matching, non-visible bilateral flow, single-dealer platforms, RFQ venues, and fragmented external hedging. For XAUUSD, LBMA data gives turnover transparency, but not a full retail-style real-time dark-pool tape. So:


Equities: delayed ATS/off-exchange share, venue concentration, off-exchange trend.

FX/XAUUSD: internalisation ratios, venue mix, quote fade, spread resilience, external hedge intensity.


That is the actual mapping. LBMA+3finra.org+3Bank for International Settlements+3



Supply & demand, order blocks, Wyckoff phases, FVG



These are formalized state features, not canonical exchange formulas.


14.1 Supply / demand zone


Define a base  $B$  followed by an impulse  $I$ . Demand zone candidate if:


$$
Range(B) < \beta , ATR
$$


$$
\frac{Close_{t+h}-High(B)}{ATR_t} > \kappa
$$


and retests remain limited. Score:


$$
SDScore = w_f Freshness + w_i ImpulseStrength + w_u UnfilledFraction - w_r RetestCount
$$


14.2 Order block


Bullish order block = last bearish candle before upward displacement + structure break. Zone:


$$
OB^{bull}=[Low_{c^*}, High_{c^*}]
$$


with score:


$$
OBScore = w_1 Displacement + w_2 BOS + w_3 ImbalanceAdjacency + w_4 VolumeConfirmation - w_5 Mitigation
$$


14.3 Wyckoff / market phases


Latent phase variable:


$$
W_t \in {\text{accumulation},\text{markup},\text{distribution},\text{markdown},\text{manipulation}}
$$


Estimate via HMM or rules over:


range compression / expansion,

swing-failure events,

volume anomalies,

CVD divergence,

profile acceptance/rejection,

trend slope and ADX.


14.4 Fair Value Gap


Bullish FVG:


$$
Low_t > High_{t-2}
$$


Gap:


$$
FVG^{bull}t = [High{t-2}, Low_t]
$$


Mitigation ratio:


$$
Mit_t=\frac{\text{filled length}}{\text{gap size}}
$$


Unmitigated fraction:


$$
1-Mit_t
$$


These constructs can be powerful, but only after you make them measurable. Before that, they are just expensive poetry. Volumetrica Trading+1



Classical technical layer



These are standardized enough to code directly.


RSI


$$
RS_t=\frac{AvgGain_t}{AvgLoss_t} ,\qquad RSI_t = 100-\frac{100}{1+RS_t}
$$


MACD


$$
MACD_t = EMA_{12}(C_t)-EMA_{26}(C_t)
$$


$$
Signal_t = EMA_9(MACD_t) ,\qquad Hist_t = MACD_t-Signal_t
$$


StochRSI


$$
StochRSI_t= 100\cdot \frac{RSI_t-\min(RSI_{t-n+1:t})} {\max(RSI_{t-n+1:t})-\min(RSI_{t-n+1:t})+\epsilon}
$$


ROC


$$
ROC_t = 100\left(\frac{C_t}{C_{t-n}}-1\right)
$$


ADX


$$
+DM_t = \max(H_t-H_{t-1},0)\cdot \mathbf{1}{H_t-H{t-1} > L_{t-1}-L_t}
$$


$$
-DM_t = \max(L_{t-1}-L_t,0)\cdot \mathbf{1}{L{t-1}-L_t > H_t-H_{t-1}}
$$


$$
TR_t=\max(H_t-L_t,\ |H_t-C_{t-1}|,\ |L_t-C_{t-1}|)
$$


After Wilder smoothing:


$$
+DI_t = 100\frac{Sm(+DM_t)}{Sm(TR_t)} ,\qquad -DI_t = 100\frac{Sm(-DM_t)}{Sm(TR_t)}
$$


$$
DX_t = 100\frac{|+DI_t-(-DI_t)|}{+DI_t+(-DI_t)+\epsilon} ,\qquad ADX_t = Sm(DX_t)
$$


These are the standard library definitions used across TA packages. fidelity.com+4ta-lib.github.io+4ta-lib.github.io+4



Correlation, pivots, support/resistance, Fibonacci, trendlines



Correlation coefficient


For return series  $x,y$  over window  $n$ :


$$
\rho_{xy,t}= \frac{\sum_{i=1}^{n}(x_i-\bar x)(y_i-\bar y)} {\sqrt{\sum_{i=1}^{n}(x_i-\bar x)^2}\sqrt{\sum_{i=1}^{n}(y_i-\bar y)^2}}
$$


Classic pivot points


$$
PP=\frac{H_{prev}+L_{prev}+C_{prev}}{3}
$$


$$
R1=2PP-L_{prev},\qquad S1=2PP-H_{prev}
$$


$$
R2=PP+(H_{prev}-L_{prev}),\qquad S2=PP-(H_{prev}-L_{prev})
$$


Support / resistance


There is no single canonical institutional formula, so use objective clustering:


$$
SR_j = \text{k-means / density cluster centers of swing extrema, profile HVNs, pivots}
$$


Fibonacci retracements


For upswing from  $L$  to  $H$ , retracement level at ratio  $r$ :


$$
Fib_r = H-r(H-L)
$$


Common  $r$ :  $0.236,0.382,0.5,0.618,0.786$ .


Trendlines


Fit on pivot highs/lows:


$$
p_t = \alpha + \beta t + \varepsilon_t
$$


using robust regression on swing points, not eyeballing like a caffeinated art critic.


Pivots are formulaic; support/resistance and trendlines are best treated as algorithmic geometry over pivots, profiles, and extrema. Encyclopedia Britannica+3fidelity.com+3Investopedia+3



Final integrated state vector



This is the piece that makes the whole stack institutional.


Define normalized features:


$$
z(x_t)=\frac{x_t-\mu_x}{\sigma_x+\epsilon}
$$


Then the integrated state vector at time  $t$  is:


$$
\mathbf{S}_t= \begin{bmatrix} z(OFI_t) \ z(CVD_t) \ z(OBI^{(1)}_t) \ z(OBI^{(K)}t) \ z(\beta_t) \ z(\lambda_t) \ z(VPIN_t) \ z(Abs_t) \ z(Repl_t) \ z(Resil_t) \ z(m_t-VWAP_t) \ z(m_t-TWAP_t) \ z(POVGap_t) \ z(RV_t) \ z(BV_t) \ z(Jump_t) \ z(FootprintDelta_t) \ z(POCDist_t) \ z(VAState_t) \ z(SweepScore_t) \ z(SpreadShock_t) \ z(DepthFade_t) \ z(SDScore_t) \ z(OBScore_t) \ z(WyckoffScore_t) \ z(FVGScore_t) \ z(RSI_t) \ z(MACD_t) \ z(StochRSI_t) \ z(ROC_t) \ z(ADX_t) \ z(\rho{xy,t}) \ z(PivotDist_t) \ z(FibDist_t) \ z(TrendSlope_t) \end{bmatrix}
$$


Now split it into three heads:


17.1 Alpha head


$$
\alpha_t = \mathbf{w}_\alpha^\top \mathbf{S}_t
$$


17.2 Execution head


$$
ExecAgg_t = \sigma!\left(\mathbf{w}_e^\top \mathbf{S}_t\right)
$$


where  $\sigma(\cdot)$  is logistic squashing into  $[0,1]$ .


17.3 Risk head


$$
Risk_t = \mathbf{w}_r^\top \mathbf{S}_t
$$


Then the live decision stack is:


$$
\text{Trade only if } \alpha_t > \theta_\alpha,\quad Risk_t < \theta_r,\quad VPIN_t < \theta_v,\quad Resil_t > \theta_{res}
$$


and size with:


$$
q_t = q_{max}\cdot f(\alpha_t)\cdot g(Risk_t) \cdot h(ExecAgg_t)
$$


That is the clean institutional architecture: signal, execution, risk all driven by the same state vector, but with different heads.

Base market variables
bid_t = best bid
ask_t = best ask
spread_t = ask_t - bid_t
mid_t = (ask_t + bid_t) / 2
relSpread_t = (ask_t - bid_t) / mid_t
bidSize_t = best bid size
askSize_t = best ask size
micro_t = (ask_t * bidSize_t + bid_t * askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t)


If venue aggregated:
midAgg_t = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * mid_{t,v}
spreadAgg_t = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * spread_{t,v}
sum_{v=1..V} w_v = 1


Depth of Market, explicit DOM
DOMBid_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t}
DOMAsk_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}
DOMTot_t^(K) = DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K)
DOMSkew_t^(K) = (DOMBid_t^(K) - DOMAsk_t^(K)) / (DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K))
DOMPressure_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * (B_{k,t} - A_{k,t})
Use w_k = exp(-alphaDepth * (k - 1)) or another monotone decay.


If OTC venue aggregated:
DOMBidAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMBid_{t,v}^(K)
DOMAskAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMAsk_{t,v}^(K)
DOMSkewAgg_t^(K) = (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) - DOMAskAgg_t^(K)) / (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) + DOMAskAgg_t^(K))


Explicit Order Flow
eps_i in {-1, +1}
flow_i = eps_i * vol_i
BuyFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = +1) * vol_i
SellFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = -1) * vol_i
NetFlow_[t-h,t] = BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]
FlowImb_[t-h,t] = (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]) / (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] + SellFlow_[t-h,t] + eps)
SignedFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} eps_i * vol_i
CVD_t = CVD_{t-1} + sum_{i in (t-dt,t]} eps_i * vol_i

Order Book Imbalance, explicit OBI
OBI_t^(1) = (bidSize_t - askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t)
OBI_t^(K) = (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} - sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}) / (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} + sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t} + eps)


If OTC venue aggregated:
OBIAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * OBI_{t,v}^(K)


Keep the alias explicit:
OBI_t^(1) = DOMSkew_t^(1)


Event-based OFI
e_n = I(P_n^B >= P_{n-1}^B) * q_n^B - I(P_n^B <= P_{n-1}^B) * q_{n-1}^B - I(P_n^A <= P_{n-1}^A) * q_n^A + I(P_n^A >= P_{n-1}^A) * q_{n-1}^A
OFI_t = sum_{n in W_t} e_n
DeltaMid_t = beta_t * OFI_t + err_t

Rolling impact coefficients
beta_t = argmin_beta sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - beta * OFI_u)^2
Qsign_u = sum_{i in u} eps_i * vol_i
lambda_t = argmin_lambda sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - lambda * Qsign_u)^2


Alternative concave-size impact form:
DeltaMid_u = lambda_t * sign(Q_u) * abs(Q_u)^psi + eta_u
0 < psi <= 1


Toxicity filter
For bucket size Vstar:
Vplus_b = buy volume in bucket b
Vminus_b = sell volume in bucket b
ImbBucket_b = abs(Vplus_b - Vminus_b) / Vstar
VPIN_t = (1 / N) * sum_{b=t-N+1..t} ImbBucket_b

Absorption, replenishment, resiliency
AbsPlus_t = VaggrPlus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
AbsMinus_t = VaggrMinus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
ReplBid_t = (DepthBid_post - DepthBid_min) / (Vsell_shock + eps)
ReplAsk_t = (DepthAsk_post - DepthAsk_min) / (Vbuy_shock + eps)
Resil_t = exp(-tau_spread / tauSpreadBar) * exp(-tau_depth / tauDepthBar)

Metaorder impact and execution
Impact_t(Q) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / V_D)
Impact_t(Q, partRate) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / V_D) * f(partRate)


Execution schedule:
min_{n_k} E(C) + LambdaRisk * Var(C)
subject to X_k = X_{k-1} - n_k, X_0 = Q, X_N = 0
S_k = S_{k-1} + sigma * sqrt(tau) * xi_k + gamma * n_k
ExecPrice_k = S_k + eta * (n_k / tau)
C = sum_{k=1..N} n_k * ExecPrice_k - Q * S_0


VWAP, TWAP, POV
VWAP_[0,T] = sum_i p_i * vol_i / sum_i vol_i
TWAP_[0,T] = (1 / N) * sum_{k=1..N} p_k
ContinuousTWAP_[0,T] = (1 / T) * integral_0^T p(t) dt
n_t = min(Xrem_t, rho * Vmarket_t)


Expected volume curve:
integral_0^T u(t) dt = 1
x'(t) = Q * u(t)


Cumulative POV target:
POVTargetCum_t = rho * CumMarketVol_t
POVGap_t = CumExec_t - POVTargetCum_t


Volatility layer
RV_t = sum_{i=1..M} r_{t,i}^2
sigmaP_t^2 = (1 / (4 * ln(2))) * (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (ln(H_j / L_j))^2
sigmaGK_t^2 = (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (0.5 * (ln(H_j / L_j))^2 - (2 * ln(2) - 1) * (ln(C_j / O_j))^2)
mu1 = sqrt(2 / pi)
BV_t = mu1^(-2) * sum_{i=2..M} abs(r_i) * abs(r_{i-1})
Jump_t = max(RV_t - BV_t, 0)

VSA
BarSpread_t = H_t - L_t
CLV_t = (2 * C_t - H_t - L_t) / (H_t - L_t + eps)
VolZ_t = (V_t - mean(V)) / (stdev(V) + eps)
ER_t = ((C_t - O_t) / (ATR_t + eps)) * VolZ_t

Footprint
Delta_{p,t} = Vplus_{p,t} - Vminus_{p,t}
FootprintDelta_t = sum_p Delta_{p,t}
ImbAsk_{p,t} = Vplus_{p,t} / (Vminus_{p-dp,t} + eps)
ImbBid_{p,t} = Vminus_{p,t} / (Vplus_{p+dp,t} + eps)
Stacked imbalance exists when k consecutive price levels satisfy ImbAsk_{p,t} > thetaImb or ImbBid_{p,t} > thetaImb

Volume Profile and Market Profile
VP_j = sum_{i : p_i in bin_j} vol_i
POC_t = argmax_j VP_j
Construct Value Area by expanding from POC until cumulative volume reaches the target fraction
POCDist_t = (C_t - POC_t) / (ATR_t + eps)
VAState_t = 1 if C_t > VAH_t, 0 if VAL_t <= C_t <= VAH_t, -1 if C_t < VAL_t


TPO_j = sum_{tau in session} I(price_tau in bin_j)
TPOState_t = 1 if C_t > TPO_VAH_t, 0 if TPO_VAL_t <= C_t <= TPO_VAH_t, -1 if C_t < TPO_VAL_t


Liquidity, hunts, stops, grabs, sweeps, spread, slippage
BullishSweep_t = 1 if L_t < SwingLowPool_t - epsL and C_t > SwingLowPool_t else 0
BearishSweep_t = 1 if H_t > SwingHighPool_t + epsL and C_t < SwingHighPool_t else 0
SweepScore_t = w1 * (ExcursionBeyondPool_t / ATR_t) + w2 * (ReversalDistance_t / ATR_t) + w3 * abs(DeltaCVD_t) + w4 * abs(OFI_t)
SpreadShock_t = (spread_t - mean(spread)) / (stdev(spread) + eps)
Slippage_t = sign(side_t) * (Pexec_t - Pdecision_t) / tick
DepthFade_t = 1 - Depth_t / (mean(Depth) + eps)

Dark pools and internalisation proxies
For equities:
DPShare_t = OffExchangeVol_t / (TotalVol_t + eps)
ATSShare_t = ATSVol_t / (TotalVol_t + eps)


For fragmented OTC FX/XAUUSD:
VenueShare_{v,t} = Vol_{v,t} / (sum_j Vol_{j,t} + eps)
VenueMixEntropy_t = -sum_v VenueShare_{v,t} * ln(VenueShare_{v,t} + eps)
InternalisationProxy_t = InternalMatchedFlow_t / (TotalObservedFlow_t + eps)


If InternalMatchedFlow_t is unavailable:
InternalisationProxy_t = 1 - ExternalHedgeFlow_t / (TotalClientFlow_t + eps)


QuoteFade_t = CancelledQuotedSize_t / (PostedQuotedSize_t + eps)


Supply and demand zones
RangeBase_t = HighBase_t - LowBase_t
DemandZoneCandidate_t = 1 if RangeBase_t < betaSD * ATR_t and (Close_{t+h} - HighBase_t) / (ATR_t + eps) > kappaSD else 0
SDScore_t = wFresh * Freshness_t + wImpulse * ImpulseStrength_t + wUnfilled * UnfilledFraction_t - wRetest * RetestCount_t

Order blocks
BullishOB_t = [Low_{cstar}, High_{cstar}]
OBScore_t = w1 * Displacement_t + w2 * BOS_t + w3 * ImbalanceAdjacency_t + w4 * VolumeConfirm_t - w5 * Mitigation_t

Wyckoff and market phases
W_t in {accumulation, markup, distribution, markdown, manipulation}


If probabilistic:
WyckoffScore_t = P_t(markup) + P_t(accumulation) - P_t(markdown) - P_t(distribution)


If rule-based:
WyckoffScore_t = mapped signed phase score in [-1, 1]


Fair Value Gaps
BullishFVG_t = 1 if Low_t > High_{t-2} else 0
BullishFVGZone_t = [High_{t-2}, Low_t]
GapSize_t = Low_t - High_{t-2}
Mit_t = FilledLength_t / (GapSize_t + eps)
UnfilledFrac_t = 1 - Mit_t
FVGScore_t = wGap * (GapSize_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + wOpen * UnfilledFrac_t + wHTF * HTFAlignment_t - wMit * Mit_t

Classical indicators
RS_t = AvgGain_t / (AvgLoss_t + eps)
RSI_t = 100 - 100 / (1 + RS_t)


MACD_t = EMA12(C_t) - EMA26(C_t)
Signal_t = EMA9(MACD_t)
Hist_t = MACD_t - Signal_t


StochRSI_t = 100 * (RSI_t - min(RSI_{t-n+1..t})) / (max(RSI_{t-n+1..t}) - min(RSI_{t-n+1..t}) + eps)


ROC_t = 100 * (C_t / C_{t-n} - 1)


PlusDM_t = max(H_t - H_{t-1}, 0) * I(H_t - H_{t-1} > L_{t-1} - L_t)
MinusDM_t = max(L_{t-1} - L_t, 0) * I(L_{t-1} - L_t > H_t - H_{t-1})
TR_t = max(H_t - L_t, abs(H_t - C_{t-1}), abs(L_t - C_{t-1}))
PlusDI_t = 100 * Sm(PlusDM_t) / (Sm(TR_t) + eps)
MinusDI_t = 100 * Sm(MinusDM_t) / (Sm(TR_t) + eps)
DX_t = 100 * abs(PlusDI_t - MinusDI_t) / (PlusDI_t + MinusDI_t + eps)
ADX_t = Sm(DX_t)


Correlation, pivot levels, support and resistance, Fibonacci, trendlines
rho_xy,t = sum_{i=1..n} (x_i - xbar) * (y_i - ybar) / (sqrt(sum_{i=1..n} (x_i - xbar)^2) * sqrt(sum_{i=1..n} (y_i - ybar)^2) + eps)


PP_t = (H_prev + L_prev + C_prev) / 3
R1_t = 2 * PP_t - L_prev
S1_t = 2 * PP_t - H_prev
R2_t = PP_t + (H_prev - L_prev)
S2_t = PP_t - (H_prev - L_prev)


Fib_r = H - r * (H - L), where r in {0.236, 0.382, 0.5, 0.618, 0.786}


Trendline regression:
price_t = a + b * t + err_t
TrendSlope_t = b


Objective support/resistance:
SR_j = cluster centers of swing extrema, profile nodes, and pivots
SRDist_t = min_j abs(C_t - SR_j) / (ATR_t + eps)
PivotDist_t = min(abs(C_t - PP_t), abs(C_t - R1_t), abs(C_t - S1_t), abs(C_t - R2_t), abs(C_t - S2_t)) / (ATR_t + eps)
FibDist_t = min_{r in R} abs(C_t - Fib_r) / (ATR_t + eps)


Normalization and final integrated state vector
z(x_t) = (x_t - mean(x)) / (stdev(x) + eps)


Use venue-aggregated versions for FX/XAUUSD where needed.

The sane institutional use is therefore: **keep VPIN-style toxicity as one filter among many**, and always pair it with better trade classification and explicit spread/depth/resiliency checks. Don’t. ### 7.1 Absorption Measure how much aggressive volume is absorbed with little price displacement: Abst+\=VWtaggr,+1+∣ΔmWt∣/δp,Abst−\=VWtaggr,−1+∣ΔmWt∣/δpAbs\_t^{+}=\\frac{V^{aggr,+}\_{W\_t}}{1+\\left|\\Delta m\_{W\_t}\\right|/\\delta p} ,\\qquad Abs\_t^{-}=\\frac{V^{aggr,-}\_{W\_t}}{1+\\left|\\Delta m\_{W\_t}\\right|/\\delta p}Abst+​\=1+∣ΔmWt​​∣/δpVWt​aggr,+​​,Abst−​\=1+∣ΔmWt​​∣/δpVWt​aggr,−​​ Large same-side aggressive volume with small move = strong absorption. ### 7.2 Replenishment After a liquidity shock, measure how much displayed depth refills: Repltb\=Dpostb−DminbVshocksell+ϵ,Replta\=Dposta−DminaVshockbuy+ϵRepl\_t^{b}=\\frac{D^{b}\_{post}-D^{b}\_{min}}{V^{sell}\_{shock}+\\epsilon} ,\\qquad Repl\_t^{a}=\\frac{D^{a}\_{post}-D^{a}\_{min}}{V^{buy}\_{shock}+\\epsilon}Repltb​\=Vshocksell​+ϵDpostb​−Dminb​​,Replta​\=Vshockbuy​+ϵDposta​−Dmina​​ ### 7.3 Resiliency Let τs\\tau\_sτs​ be the time for spread to revert to baseline and τd\\tau\_dτd​ the time for depth to revert. Then a compact resiliency score is: Resilt\=exp⁡ ⁣(−τsτˉs)exp⁡ ⁣(−τdτˉd)Resil\_t=\\exp\\!\\left(-\\frac{\\tau\_s}{\\bar\\tau\_s}\\right)\\exp\\!\\left(-\\frac{\\tau\_d}{\\bar\\tau\_d}\\right)Resilt​\=exp(−τˉs​τs​​)exp(−τˉd​τd​​) or, if you estimate impulse responses directly, use half-lives from the spread/depth/intensity response. The literature on order-book resiliency is explicit: liquidity shocks widen/spoil liquidity immediately, then replenishment speed matters enormously. [ScienceDirect+1](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0165188915001797) * * * 8) Metaorder impact for execution --------------------------------- For a parent order of size QQQ, daily volume VDV\_DVD​, and volatility σD\\sigma\_DσD​, the public institutional benchmark is the **square-root impact law**: I(Q)≈Y σDQVDI(Q)\\approx Y\\,\\sigma\_D \\sqrt{\\frac{Q}{V\_D}}I(Q)≈YσD​VD​Q​​ A participation-aware version is: I(Q,γ)≈Y σDQVD f(γ)I(Q,\\gamma)\\approx Y\\,\\sigma\_D \\sqrt{\\frac{Q}{V\_D}}\\,f(\\gamma)I(Q,γ)≈YσD​VD​Q​​f(γ) where γ\\gammaγ is participation rate. Recent work continues to support square-root scaling at the metaorder level, with participation effects layered on top. This is execution math, not social-media incense. [Taylor & Francis Online+2EconPapers+2](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/14697688.2013.848464) For explicit scheduling, use an Almgren–Chriss-style objective: min⁡{nk}E\[C\]+Λ Var(C)\\min\_{\\{n\_k\\}} \\mathbb{E}\[C\]+\\Lambda\\,\\mathrm{Var}(C){nk​}min​E\[C\]+ΛVar(C) subject to: Xk\=Xk−1−nk,X0\=Q,XN\=0X\_k=X\_{k-1}-n\_k,\\qquad X\_0=Q,\\qquad X\_N=0Xk​\=Xk−1​−nk​,X0​\=Q,XN​\=0 with price dynamics: Sk\=Sk−1+στ ξk+γnkS\_k=S\_{k-1}+\\sigma \\sqrt{\\tau}\\,\\xi\_k+\\gamma n\_kSk​\=Sk−1​+στ​ξk​+γnk​ execution price: Sk\=Sk+ηnkτ\\tilde S\_k=S\_k+\\eta \\frac{n\_k}{\\tau}Sk​\=Sk​+ητnk​​ and cost: C\=∑k\=1NnkSk−QS0C=\\sum\_{k=1}^{N} n\_k \\tilde S\_k - Q S\_0C\=k\=1∑N​nk​Sk​−QS0​ That gives you the implementation-shortfall backbone. [Econometric Society+2Mathematical Institute, Oxford+2](https://www.econometricsociety.org/publications/econometrica/1985/11/01/continuous-auctions-and-insider-trading) * * * 9) VWAP, TWAP, POV ------------------ ### VWAP benchmark VWAP\[0,T\]\=∑ipivi∑iviVWAP\_{\[0,T\]}=\\frac{\\sum\_{i} p\_i v\_i}{\\sum\_i v\_i}VWAP\[0,T\]​\=∑i​vi​∑i​pi​vi​​ ### TWAP benchmark TWAP\[0,T\]\=1N∑k\=1NpkTWAP\_{\[0,T\]}=\\frac{1}{N}\\sum\_{k=1}^{N} p\_kTWAP\[0,T\]​\=N1​k\=1∑N​pk​ or in continuous time: TWAP\=1T∫0Tp(t) dtTWAP=\\frac{1}{T}\\int\_0^T p(t)\\,dtTWAP\=T1​∫0T​p(t)dt ### POV schedule With participation rate ρ\\rhoρ: nt\=min⁡(Xrem,t,ρ Vtmkt)n\_t=\\min\\left(X\_{rem,t}, \\rho\\,V^{mkt}\_t\\right)nt​\=min(Xrem,t​,ρVtmkt​) ### Schedule targets If u(t)u(t)u(t) is the expected intraday volume curve: ∫0Tu(t) dt\=1,x′(t)\=Q u(t)\\int\_0^T u(t)\\,dt=1,\\qquad x'(t)=Q\\,u(t)∫0T​u(t)dt\=1,x′(t)\=Qu(t) Kato shows VWAP is optimal for a risk-neutral trader in an Almgren–Chriss setting with a volume process; TWAP is the flat-time benchmark; POV is the “stay a fixed fraction of market flow” schedule. In practice, serious execution stacks switch among these depending on urgency, toxicity, spread, and liquidity refill. [J-STAGE+2Mathematical Institute, Oxford+2](https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/jsiaml/7/0/7\_33/\_article) * * * 10) Volatility layer -------------------- Use **four** estimators, not one. ### 10.1 Realized variance RVt\=∑i\=1Mrt,i2RV\_t=\\sum\_{i=1}^{M} r\_{t,i}^2RVt​\=i\=1∑M​rt,i2​ ### 10.2 Parkinson range estimator σP,t2\=14ln⁡2⋅1n∑j\=1n(ln⁡HjLj)2\\sigma^2\_{P,t}= \\frac{1}{4\\ln 2}\\cdot \\frac{1}{n}\\sum\_{j=1}^{n}\\left(\\ln\\frac{H\_j}{L\_j}\\right)^2σP,t2​\=4ln21​⋅n1​j\=1∑n​(lnLj​Hj​​)2 ### 10.3 Garman–Klass σGK,t2\=1n∑j\=1n\[12(ln⁡HjLj)2−(2ln⁡2−1)(ln⁡CjOj)2\]\\sigma^2\_{GK,t} = \\frac{1}{n}\\sum\_{j=1}^{n} \\left\[ \\frac{1}{2}\\left(\\ln\\frac{H\_j}{L\_j}\\right)^2 -(2\\ln 2-1)\\left(\\ln\\frac{C\_j}{O\_j}\\right)^2 \\right\]σGK,t2​\=n1​j\=1∑n​\[21​(lnLj​Hj​​)2−(2ln2−1)(lnOj​Cj​​)2\] ### 10.4 Bipower variation BVt\=μ1−2∑i\=2M∣ri∣∣ri−1∣,μ1\=2πBV\_t=\\mu\_1^{-2}\\sum\_{i=2}^{M}|r\_i||r\_{i-1}|, \\qquad \\mu\_1=\\sqrt{\\frac{2}{\\pi}}BVt​\=μ1−2​i\=2∑M​∣ri​∣∣ri−1​∣,μ1​\=π2​​ Use RV−BVRV-BVRV−BV as a jump proxy. Range-based estimators are often more efficient than simple close-to-close measures, while bipower variation is useful for separating continuous variation from jumps. [Cambridge University Press & Assessment+3EconPapers+3revstat.ine.pt+3](https://econpapers.repec.org/RePEc%3Aucp%3Ajnlbus%3Av%3A53%3Ay%3A1980%3Ai%3A1%3Ap%3A61-65) * * * 11) VSA, footprint, volume profile, market profile -------------------------------------------------- These are **not** exchange-native primitives in OTC FX, so formalize them. ### 11.1 VSA quant layer For bar ttt: high ∣VolZ∣|VolZ|∣VolZ∣ with low normalized range or low net progress That is the quant way to make VSA testable. ### 11.2 Footprint At price level ppp within bar ttt: threshold θ\\thetaθ. ### 11.3 Volume profile For price bins jjj: VPj\=∑i: pi∈binjviVP\_j=\\sum\_{i:\\,p\_i \\in bin\_j} v\_iVPj​\=i:pi​∈binj​∑​vi​ POC\=arg⁡max⁡jVPjPOC=\\arg\\max\_j VP\_jPOC\=argjmax​VPj​ Construct Value Area by expanding outward from POCPOCPOC until cumulative volume reaches target fraction, usually 70%. ### 11.4 Market profile (TPO) TPOj\=∑τ∈session1{priceτ∈binj}TPO\_j=\\sum\_{\\tau \\in session}\\mathbf{1}\_{\\{price\_\\tau \\in bin\_j\\}}TPOj​\=τ∈session∑​1{priceτ​∈binj​}​ Volume Profile gives **where volume traded**; Market A price level becomes a liquidity pool if repeated pivots lie within tolerance band ϵL\\epsilon\_LϵL​. ### 12.2 Sweep / stop run Bullish sweep of lows: Lt<SL−ϵLandCt\>SLL\_t < SL - \\epsilon\_L \\quad\\text{and}\\quad C\_t > SLLt​<SL−ϵL​andCt​\>SL Bearish sweep of highs: Ht\>SH+ϵLandCt<SHH\_t > SH + \\epsilon\_L \\quad\\text{and}\\quad C\_t < SHHt​\>SH+ϵL​andCt​<SH Excursion score: SweepScoret\=w1excursion beyond poolATRt+w2reversal distanceATRt+w3∣ΔCVDt∣+w4∣OFIt∣SweepScore\_t= w\_1\\frac{\\text{excursion beyond pool}}{ATR\_t} +w\_2\\frac{\\text{reversal distance}}{ATR\_t} +w\_3|\\Delta CVD\_t| +w\_4|OFI\_t|SweepScoret​\=w1​ATRt​excursion beyond pool​+w2​ATRt​reversal distance​+w3​∣ΔCVDt​∣+w4​∣OFIt​∣ ### 12.3 Spread shock SpreadShockt\=st−μsσsSpreadShock\_t=\\frac{s\_t-\\mu\_s}{\\sigma\_s}SpreadShockt​\=σs​st​−μs​​ ### 12.4 Slippage Slippage\=signed(side)⋅Pexec−PdecisiontickSlippage = \\text{signed}(side)\\cdot \\frac{P\_{exec}-P\_{decision}}{tick}Slippage\=signed(side)⋅tickPexec​−Pdecision​​ ### 12.5 Depth fade DepthFadet\=1−DtμDDepthFade\_t = 1 - \\frac{D\_t}{\\mu\_D}DepthFadet​\=1−μD​Dt​​ For FX/XAUUSD, spread changes, quote fade, slippage, and venue-to-venue dislocations are often more observable than “real” consolidated stop-flow. So code what the market actually gives you, not what YouTube promised you. [Bank for International Settlements+2Bank for International Settlements+2](https://www.bis.org/publ/qtrpdf/r\_qt2512.pdf) * * * 13) Dark pools -------------- For **U.S. equities**, dark/off-exchange flow can be proxied with FINRA ATS/OTC data, but it is **delayed**, not a real-time execution edge by itself. For **spot FX**, “dark pool” is the wrong mental model much of the time; the better framework is **internalisation**, dealer inventory matching, non-visible bilateral flow, single-dealer platforms, RFQ venues, and fragmented external hedging. For **XAUUSD**, LBMA data gives turnover transparency, but not a full retail-style real-time dark-pool tape. So: * **Equities**: delayed ATS/off-exchange share, venue concentration, off-exchange trend. * **FX/XAUUSD**: internalisation ratios, venue mix, quote fade, spread resilience, external hedge intensity. That is the actual mapping. [fidelity.com+4ta-lib.github.io+4ta-lib.github.io+4](https://ta-lib.github.io/ta-doc/indicator/RSI.htm) * * * 16) Correlation, pivots, support/resistance, Fibonacci, trendlines ------------------------------------------------------------------ ### Correlation coefficient For return series x,yx,yx,y over window nnn: ρxy,t\=∑i\=1n(xi−xˉ)(yi−yˉ)∑i\=1n(xi−xˉ)2∑i\=1n(yi−yˉ)2\\rho\_{xy,t}= \\frac{\\sum\_{i=1}^{n}(x\_i-\\bar x)(y\_i-\\bar y)} {\\sqrt{\\sum\_{i=1}^{n}(x\_i-\\bar x)^2}\\sqrt{\\sum\_{i=1}^{n}(y\_i-\\bar y)^2}}ρxy,t​\=∑i\=1n​(xi​−xˉ)2​∑i\=1n​(yi​−yˉ​)2​∑i\=1n​(xi​−xˉ)(yi​−yˉ​)​ ### Classic pivot points PP\=Hprev+Lprev+Cprev3PP=\\frac{H\_{prev}+L\_{prev}+C\_{prev}}{3}PP\=3Hprev​+Lprev​+Cprev​​ R1\=2PP−Lprev,S1\=2PP−HprevR1=2PP-L\_{prev},\\qquad S1=2PP-H\_{prev}R1\=2PP−Lprev​,S1\=2PP−Hprev​ R2\=PP+(Hprev−Lprev),S2\=PP−(Hprev−Lprev)R2=PP+(H\_{prev}-L\_{prev}),\\qquad S2=PP-(H\_{prev}-L\_{prev})R2\=PP+(Hprev​−Lprev​),S2\=PP−(Hprev​−Lprev​) ### Support / resistance There is no single canonical institutional formula, so use objective clustering: SRj\=k-means / density cluster centers of swing extrema, profile HVNs, pivotsSR\_j = \\text{k-means / density cluster centers of swing extrema, profile HVNs, pivots}SRj​\=k-means / density cluster centers of swing extrema, profile HVNs, pivots ### Fibonacci retracements For upswing from LLL to HHH, retracement level at ratio rrr: Fibr\=H−r(H−L)Fib\_r = H-r(H-L)Fibr​\=H−r(H−L) Common rrr: 0.236,0.382,0.5,0.618,0.7860.236,0.382,0.5,0.618,0.7860.236,0.382,0.5,0.618,0.786. ### Trendlines Fit on pivot highs/lows: pt\=α+βt+εtp\_t = \\alpha + \\beta t + \\varepsilon\_tpt​\=α+βt+εt​ using robust regression on swing points, not eyeballing like a caffeinated art critic. Base market variables
bid_t = best bid
ask_t = best ask
spread_t = ask_t - bid_t
mid_t = (ask_t + bid_t) / 2
relSpread_t = (ask_t - bid_t) / mid_t
bidSize_t = best bid size
askSize_t = best ask size
micro_t = (ask_t * bidSize_t + bid_t * askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t)


If venue aggregated:
midAgg_t = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * mid_{t,v}
spreadAgg_t = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * spread_{t,v}
sum_{v=1..V} w_v = 1


Depth of Market, explicit DOM
DOMBid_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t}
DOMAsk_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}
DOMTot_t^(K) = DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K)
DOMSkew_t^(K) = (DOMBid_t^(K) - DOMAsk_t^(K)) / (DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K))
DOMPressure_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * (B_{k,t} - A_{k,t})
Use w_k = exp(-alphaDepth * (k - 1)) or another monotone decay.


If OTC venue aggregated:
DOMBidAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMBid_{t,v}^(K)
DOMAskAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMAsk_{t,v}^(K)
DOMSkewAgg_t^(K) = (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) - DOMAskAgg_t^(K)) / (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) + DOMAskAgg_t^(K))


Explicit Order Flow
eps_i in {-1, +1}
flow_i = eps_i * vol_i
BuyFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = +1) * vol_i
SellFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = -1) * vol_i
NetFlow_[t-h,t] = BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]
FlowImb_[t-h,t] = (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]) / (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] + SellFlow_[t-h,t] + eps)
SignedFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} eps_i * vol_i
CVD_t = CVD_{t-1} + sum_{i in (t-dt,t]} eps_i * vol_i
Order Book Imbalance, explicit OBI
OBI_t^(1) = (bidSize_t - askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t)
OBI_t^(K) = (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} - sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}) / (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} + sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t} + eps)


If OTC venue aggregated:
OBIAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * OBI_{t,v}^(K)


Keep the alias explicit:
OBI_t^(1) = DOMSkew_t^(1)


Event-based OFI
e_n = I(P_n^B >= P_{n-1}^B) * q_n^B - I(P_n^B <= P_{n-1}^B) * q_{n-1}^B - I(P_n^A <= P_{n-1}^A) * q_n^A + I(P_n^A >= P_{n-1}^A) * q_{n-1}^A
OFI_t = sum_{n in W_t} e_n
DeltaMid_t = beta_t * OFI_t + err_t
Rolling impact coefficients
beta_t = argmin_beta sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - beta * OFI_u)^2
Qsign_u = sum_{i in u} eps_i * vol_i
lambda_t = argmin_lambda sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - lambda * Qsign_u)^2


Alternative concave-size impact form:
DeltaMid_u = lambda_t * sign(Q_u) * abs(Q_u)^psi + eta_u
0 < psi <= 1


Toxicity filter
For bucket size Vstar:
Vplus_b = buy volume in bucket b
Vminus_b = sell volume in bucket b
ImbBucket_b = abs(Vplus_b - Vminus_b) / Vstar
VPIN_t = (1 / N) * sum_{b=t-N+1..t} ImbBucket_b
Absorption, replenishment, resiliency
AbsPlus_t = VaggrPlus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
AbsMinus_t = VaggrMinus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
ReplBid_t = (DepthBid_post - DepthBid_min) / (Vsell_shock + eps)
ReplAsk_t = (DepthAsk_post - DepthAsk_min) / (Vbuy_shock + eps)
Resil_t = exp(-tau_spread / tauSpreadBar) * exp(-tau_depth / tauDepthBar)
Metaorder impact and execution
Impact_t(Q) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / V_D)
Impact_t(Q, partRate) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / V_D) * f(partRate)


Execution schedule:
min_{n_k} E(C) + LambdaRisk * Var(C)
subject to X_k = X_{k-1} - n_k, X_0 = Q, X_N = 0
S_k = S_{k-1} + sigma * sqrt(tau) * xi_k + gamma * n_k
ExecPrice_k = S_k + eta * (n_k / tau)
C = sum_{k=1..N} n_k * ExecPrice_k - Q * S_0


VWAP, TWAP, POV
VWAP_[0,T] = sum_i p_i * vol_i / sum_i vol_i
TWAP_[0,T] = (1 / N) * sum_{k=1..N} p_k
ContinuousTWAP_[0,T] = (1 / T) * integral_0^T p(t) dt
n_t = min(Xrem_t, rho * Vmarket_t)


Expected volume curve:
integral_0^T u(t) dt = 1
x'(t) = Q * u(t)


Cumulative POV target:
POVTargetCum_t = rho * CumMarketVol_t
POVGap_t = CumExec_t - POVTargetCum_t


Volatility layer
RV_t = sum_{i=1..M} r_{t,i}^2
sigmaP_t^2 = (1 / (4 * ln(2))) * (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (ln(H_j / L_j))^2
sigmaGK_t^2 = (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (0.5 * (ln(H_j / L_j))^2 - (2 * ln(2) - 1) * (ln(C_j / O_j))^2)
mu1 = sqrt(2 / pi)
BV_t = mu1^(-2) * sum_{i=2..M} abs(r_i) * abs(r_{i-1})
Jump_t = max(RV_t - BV_t, 0)
VSA
BarSpread_t = H_t - L_t
CLV_t = (2 * C_t - H_t - L_t) / (H_t - L_t + eps)
VolZ_t = (V_t - mean(V)) / (stdev(V) + eps)
ER_t = ((C_t - O_t) / (ATR_t + eps)) * VolZ_t
Footprint
Delta_{p,t} = Vplus_{p,t} - Vminus_{p,t}
FootprintDelta_t = sum_p Delta_{p,t}
ImbAsk_{p,t} = Vplus_{p,t} / (Vminus_{p-dp,t} + eps)
ImbBid_{p,t} = Vminus_{p,t} / (Vplus_{p+dp,t} + eps)
Stacked imbalance exists when k consecutive price levels satisfy ImbAsk_{p,t} > thetaImb or ImbBid_{p,t} > thetaImb
Volume Profile and Market Profile
VP_j = sum_{i : p_i in bin_j} vol_i
POC_t = argmax_j VP_j
Construct Value Area by expanding from POC until cumulative volume reaches the target fraction
POCDist_t = (C_t - POC_t) / (ATR_t + eps)
VAState_t = 1 if C_t > VAH_t, 0 if VAL_t <= C_t <= VAH_t, -1 if C_t < VAL_t


TPO_j = sum_{tau in session} I(price_tau in bin_j)
TPOState_t = 1 if C_t > TPO_VAH_t, 0 if TPO_VAL_t <= C_t <= TPO_VAH_t, -1 if C_t < TPO_VAL_t


Liquidity, hunts, stops, grabs, sweeps, spread, slippage
BullishSweep_t = 1 if L_t < SwingLowPool_t - epsL and C_t > SwingLowPool_t else 0
BearishSweep_t = 1 if H_t > SwingHighPool_t + epsL and C_t < SwingHighPool_t else 0
SweepScore_t = w1 * (ExcursionBeyondPool_t / ATR_t) + w2 * (ReversalDistance_t / ATR_t) + w3 * abs(DeltaCVD_t) + w4 * abs(OFI_t)
SpreadShock_t = (spread_t - mean(spread)) / (stdev(spread) + eps)
Slippage_t = sign(side_t) * (Pexec_t - Pdecision_t) / tick
DepthFade_t = 1 - Depth_t / (mean(Depth) + eps)

DPShare_t = OffExchangeVol_t / (TotalVol_t + eps)
ATSShare_t = ATSVol_t / (TotalVol_t + eps)

InternalisationProxy_t = InternalMatchedFlow_t / (TotalObservedFlow_t + eps)


If InternalMatchedFlow_t is unavailable:

RangeBase_t = HighBase_t - LowBase_t

SDScore_t = wFresh * Freshness_t + wImpulse * ImpulseStrength_t + wUnfilled * UnfilledFraction_t - wRetest * RetestCount_t
Order blocks

W_t in {accumulation, markup, distribution, markdown, manipulation}

Base market variables
bid_t = best bid

Institutional category signal bank

2.1 Order Books and explicit Depth of Market
DOMBid_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t}
DOMAsk_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}
DOMTot_t^(K) = DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K)
DOMSkew_t^(K) = (DOMBid_t^(K) - DOMAsk_t^(K)) / (DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K) + eps)
DOMPressure_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * (B_{k,t} - A_{k,t})
Use w_k = exp(-alphaDepth * (k - 1)) or another monotone decay.


If OTC venue aggregated:
DOMBidAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMBid_{t,v}^(K)
DOMAskAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMAsk_{t,v}^(K)
DOMSkewAgg_t^(K) = (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) - DOMAskAgg_t^(K)) / (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) + DOMAskAgg_t^(K) + eps)


2.2 Explicit Order Flow
eps_i in {-1, +1}
flow_i = eps_i * vol_i
BuyFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = +1) * vol_i
SellFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = -1) * vol_i
NetFlow_[t-h,t] = BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]
FlowImb_[t-h,t] = (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]) / (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] + SellFlow_[t-h,t] + eps)
SignedFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} eps_i * vol_i
CVD_t = CVD_{t-1} + sum_{i in (t-dt,t]} eps_i * vol_i


2.3 Order Book Imbalance, explicit OBI
OBI_t^(1) = (bidSize_t - askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t + eps)
OBI_t^(K) = (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} - sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}) / (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} + sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t} + eps)


If OTC venue aggregated:
OBIAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * OBI_{t,v}^(K)


Keep the alias explicit:
OBI_t^(1) = DOMSkew_t^(1)


2.4 Event-based OFI
e_n = I(P_n^B >= P_{n-1}^B) * q_n^B - I(P_n^B <= P_{n-1}^B) * q_{n-1}^B - I(P_n^A <= P_{n-1}^A) * q_n^A + I(P_n^A >= P_{n-1}^A) * q_{n-1}^A
OFI_t = sum_{n in W_t} e_n
DeltaMid_t = beta_t * OFI_t + err_t


2.5 Rolling impact coefficients
beta_t = argmin_beta sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - beta * OFI_u)^2
Qsign_u = sum_{i in u} eps_i * vol_i
lambda_t = argmin_lambda sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - lambda * Qsign_u)^2


Alternative concave-size impact form:
DeltaMid_u = lambda_t * sign(Q_u) * abs(Q_u)^psi + eta_u
0 < psi <= 1


2.6 Toxicity filter
For bucket size Vstar:
Vplus_b = buy volume in bucket b
Vminus_b = sell volume in bucket b
ImbBucket_b = abs(Vplus_b - Vminus_b) / Vstar
VPIN_t = (1 / N) * sum_{b=t-N+1..t} ImbBucket_b


2.7 Absorption, replenishment, resiliency
AbsPlus_t = VaggrPlus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
AbsMinus_t = VaggrMinus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
ReplBid_t = (DepthBid_post - DepthBid_min) / (Vsell_shock + eps)
ReplAsk_t = (DepthAsk_post - DepthAsk_min) / (Vbuy_shock + eps)
Resil_t = exp(-tau_spread / tauSpreadBar) * exp(-tau_depth / tauDepthBar)


2.8 Metaorder impact and execution
Impact_t(Q) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / (V_D + eps))
Impact_t(Q, partRate) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / (V_D + eps)) * f(partRate)


Execution schedule:
min_{n_k} E(C) + LambdaRisk * Var(C)
subject to X_k = X_{k-1} - n_k, X_0 = Q, X_N = 0
S_k = S_{k-1} + sigma * sqrt(tau) * xi_k + gamma * n_k
ExecPrice_k = S_k + eta * (n_k / tau)
C = sum_{k=1..N} n_k * ExecPrice_k - Q * S_0


2.9 VWAP, TWAP, POV
VWAP_[0,T] = sum_i p_i * vol_i / (sum_i vol_i + eps)
TWAP_[0,T] = (1 / N) * sum_{k=1..N} p_k
ContinuousTWAP_[0,T] = (1 / T) * integral_0^T p(t) dt
n_t = min(Xrem_t, rho * Vmarket_t)


Expected volume curve:
integral_0^T u(t) dt = 1
x'(t) = Q * u(t)


Cumulative POV target:
POVTargetCum_t = rho * CumMarketVol_t
POVGap_t = CumExec_t - POVTargetCum_t

PlusDM_t = max(H_t - H_{t-1}, 0) * I(H_t - H_{t-1} > L_{t-1} - L_t)
MinusDM_t = max(L_{t-1} - L_t, 0) * I(L_{t-1} - L_t > H_t - H_{t-1})

MinusDI_t = 100 * Sm(MinusDM_t) / (Sm(TR_t) + eps)

W_t in {accumulation, markup, distribution, markdown, manipulation}
