ExternalHedgeFlow\_t / (TotalClientFlow\_t + eps) QuoteFade\_t = CancelledQuotedSize\_t / (PostedQuotedSize\_t + eps) 3. Trend category signal bank Trend candidates are computed separately and only one becomes the active Trend category signal in CategorySelectedVector\_t. 3.1 VWAP and TWAP trend distance VWAPDist\_t = mid\_t - VWAP\_t TWAPDist\_t = mid\_t - TWAP\_t 3.2 EMAs alpha\_n = 2 / (n + 1) EMA\_n,t = alpha\_n \* C\_t + (1 - alpha\_n) \* EMA\_n,t-1 EMAFast\_t = EMA\_{nFast,t} EMASlow\_t = EMA\_{nSlow,t} EMASpread\_t = EMAFast\_t - EMASlow\_t EMASlope\_t = EMA\_n,t - EMA\_n,t-1 3.3 ADX trend strength PlusDM\_t = max(H\_t - H\_{t-1}, 0) \* I(H\_t - H\_{t-1} > L\_{t-1} - L\_t) MinusDM\_t = max(L\_{t-1} - L\_t, 0) \* I(L\_{t-1} - L\_t > H\_t - H\_{t-1}) TR\_t = max(H\_t - L\_t, abs(H\_t - C\_{t-1}), abs(L\_t - C\_{t-1})) PlusDI\_t = 100 \* Sm(PlusDM\_t) / (Sm(TR\_t) + eps) MinusDI\_t = 100 \* Sm(MinusDM\_t) / (Sm(TR\_t) + eps) DX\_t = 100 \* abs(PlusDI\_t - MinusDI\_t) / (PlusDI\_t + MinusDI\_t + eps) ADX\_t = Sm(DX\_t) 3.4 Trendline regression price\_t = a + b \* t + err\_t TrendSlope\_t = b 4. Momentum category signal bank Momentum candidates are computed separately and only one becomes the active Momentum category signal in CategorySelectedVector\_t. 4.1 RSI RS\_t = AvgGain\_t / (AvgLoss\_t + eps) RSI\_t = 100 - 100 / (1 + RS\_t) 4.2 MACD MACD\_t = EMA12(C\_t) - EMA26(C\_t) Signal\_t = EMA9(MACD\_t) Hist\_t = MACD\_t - Signal\_t 4.3 StochRSI StochRSI\_t = 100 \* (RSI\_t - min(RSI\_{t-n+1..t})) / (max(RSI\_{t-n+1..t}) - min(RSI\_{t-n+1..t}) + eps) 4.4 ROC ROC\_t = 100 \* (C\_t / (C\_{t-n} + eps) - 1) 5. Volume category signal bank Volume candidates are computed separately and only one becomes the active Volume category signal in CategorySelectedVector\_t. 5.1 VSA BarSpread\_t = H\_t - L\_t CLV\_t = (2 \* C\_t - H\_t - L\_t) / (H\_t - L\_t + eps) VolZ\_t = (V\_t - mean(V)) / (stdev(V) + eps) ER\_t = ((C\_t - O\_t) / (ATR\_t + eps)) \* VolZ\_t 5.2 Footprint Delta\_{p,t} = Vplus\_{p,t} - Vminus\_{p,t} FootprintDelta\_t = sum\_p Delta\_{p,t} ImbAsk\_{p,t} = Vplus\_{p,t} / (Vminus\_{p-dp,t} + eps) ImbBid\_{p,t} = Vminus\_{p,t} / (Vplus\_{p+dp,t} + eps) StackedImbalance\_t = 1 if there exist k consecutive price levels such that ImbAsk\_{p,t} > thetaImb or ImbBid\_{p,t} > thetaImb else 0 5.3 Volume Profile and Market Profile VP\_j = sum\_{i : p\_i in bin\_j} vol\_i POC\_t = argmax\_j VP\_j Construct Value Area by expanding from POC until cumulative volume reaches the target fraction POCDist\_t = (C\_t - POC\_t) / (ATR\_t + eps) VAState\_t = 1 if C\_t > VAH\_t, 0 if VAL\_t <= C\_t <= VAH\_t, -1 if C\_t < VAL\_t TPO\_j = sum\_{tau in session} I(price\_tau in bin\_j) TPOState\_t = 1 if C\_t > TPO\_VAH\_t, 0 if TPO\_VAL\_t <= C\_t <= TPO\_VAH\_t, -1 if C\_t < TPO\_VAL\_t 6. Volatility category signal bank Volatility candidates are computed separately and only one becomes the active Volatility category signal in CategorySelectedVector\_t. 6.1 Realized and range-based volatility RV\_t = sum\_{i=1..M} r\_{t,i}^2 sigmaP\_t^2 = (1 / (4 \* ln(2))) \* (1 / n) \* sum\_{j=1..n} (ln(H\_j / L\_j))^2 sigmaP\_t = sqrt(max(sigmaP\_t^2, 0)) sigmaGK\_t^2 = (1 / n) \* sum\_{j=1..n} (0.5 \* (ln(H\_j / L\_j))^2 - (2 \* ln(2) - 1) \* (ln(C\_j / O\_j))^2) sigmaGK\_t = sqrt(max(sigmaGK\_t^2, 0)) mu1 = sqrt(2 / pi) BV\_t = mu1^(-2) \* sum\_{i=2..M} abs(r\_i) \* abs(r\_{i-1}) Jump\_t = max(RV\_t - BV\_t, 0) 6.2 ATR ATR\_t = Sm(TR\_t) 6.3 Bollinger Bands SMA\_n,t = (1 / n) \* sum\_{i=0..n-1} C\_{t-i} Std\_n,t = sqrt((1 / n) \* sum\_{i=0..n-1} (C\_{t-i} - SMA\_n,t)^2) BBUpper\_t = SMA\_n,t + kBB \* Std\_n,t BBLower\_t = SMA\_n,t - kBB \* Std\_n,t BBWidth\_t = (BBUpper\_t - BBLower\_t) / (SMA\_n,t + eps) BBPos\_t = (C\_t - BBLower\_t) / (BBUpper\_t - BBLower\_t + eps) 7. Market and Institutional Structures These are always computed and always appended to FinalIntegratedStateVector\_t. They are not subject to one-of-N category selection. 7.1 Liquidity, hunts, stops, grabs, sweeps, spread, slippage, depth fade BullishSweep\_t = 1 if L\_t < SwingLowPool\_t - epsL and C\_t > SwingLowPool\_t else 0 BearishSweep\_t = 1 if H\_t > SwingHighPool\_t + epsL and C\_t < SwingHighPool\_t else 0 SweepScore\_t = w1 \* (ExcursionBeyondPool\_t / (ATR\_t + eps)) + w2 \* (ReversalDistance\_t / (ATR\_t + eps)) + w3 \* abs(DeltaCVD\_t) + w4 \* abs(OFI\_t) SpreadShock\_t = (spread\_t - mean(spread)) / (stdev(spread) + eps) Slippage\_t = sign(side\_t) \* (Pexec\_t - Pdecision\_t) / tick DepthFade\_t = 1 - Depth\_t / (mean(Depth) + eps) 7.2 Supply and Demand zones RangeBase\_t = HighBase\_t - LowBase\_t DemandZoneCandidate\_t = 1 if RangeBase\_t < betaSD \* ATR\_t and (Close\_{t+h} - HighBase\_t) / (ATR\_t + eps) > kappaSD else 0 SDScore\_t = wFresh \* Freshness\_t + wImpulse \* ImpulseStrength\_t + wUnfilled \* UnfilledFraction\_t - wRetest \* RetestCount\_t 7.3 Order Blocks BullishOB\_t = \[Low\_{cstar}, High\_{cstar}\] OBScore\_t = w1 \* Displacement\_t + w2 \* BOS\_t + w3 \* ImbalanceAdjacency\_t + w4 \* VolumeConfirm\_t - w5 \* Mitigation\_t 7.4 Wyckoff and market phases W\_t in {accumulation, markup, distribution, markdown, manipulation} If probabilistic: WyckoffScore\_t = P\_t(markup) + P\_t(accumulation) - P\_t(markdown) - P\_t(distribution) If rule-based: WyckoffScore\_t = mapped signed phase score in \[-1, 1\] 7.5 Fair Value Gaps BullishFVG\_t = 1 if Low\_t > High\_{t-2} else 0 BullishFVGZone\_t = \[High\_{t-2}, Low\_t\] GapSize\_t = Low\_t - High\_{t-2} Mit\_t = FilledLength\_t / (GapSize\_t + eps) UnfilledFrac\_t = 1 - Mit\_t FVGScore\_t = wGap \* (GapSize\_t / (ATR\_t + eps)) + wOpen \* UnfilledFrac\_t + wHTF \* HTFAlignment\_t - wMit \* Mit\_t 7.6 Pivot levels PP\_t = (H\_prev + L\_prev + C\_prev) / 3 R1\_t = 2 \* PP\_t - L\_prev S1\_t = 2 \* PP\_t - H\_prev R2\_t = PP\_t + (H\_prev - L\_prev) S2\_t = PP\_t - (H\_prev - L\_prev) PivotDist\_t = min(abs(C\_t - PP\_t), abs(C\_t - R1\_t), abs(C\_t - S1\_t), abs(C\_t - R2\_t), abs(C\_t - S2\_t)) / (ATR\_t + eps) 7.7 Objective Support and Resistance SR\_j = cluster centers of swing extrema, profile nodes, and pivots SRDist\_t = min\_j abs(C\_t - SR\_j) / (ATR\_t + eps) 7.8 Fibonacci Fib\_r = H - r \* (H - L), where r in {0.236, 0.382, 0.5, 0.618, 0.786} FibDist\_t = min\_{r in R} abs(C\_t - Fib\_r) / (ATR\_t + eps) 7.9 Trendlines price\_t = a + b \* t + err\_t TrendSlope\_t = b 7.10 Correlation rho\_xy,t = sum\_{i=1..n} (x\_i - This video breaks down a common trap traders fall into when using multiple indicators and outlines a step-by-step framework for building a more effective, high-probability trading strategy.


Here is a detailed breakdown of the core concepts explained in the video:


The Trap of "Echo" vs. "Confluence"


The video starts by highlighting why visually perfect trading setups often fail: traders confuse "echo" with "confluence" [00:15].


The Echo Chamber: Many retail traders stack indicators that measure the exact same thing. For example, using RSI, MACD, and Stochastic together doesn't give you three unique opinions; it gives you one opinion echoed three times because they all measure momentum based on the same price data [01:48].

The Football Analogy: The narrator compares this to a football team putting five strikers in the exact same spot. It looks like you have five options, but if the goalkeeper makes a save, all five fail at once. When a setup breaks, overlapping indicators will fail together with zero warning.


The Four Categories of Indicators


To fix this, you must understand that true confirmation comes from indicators answering different questions [03:27]. Every indicator falls into one of four categories:


Trend (Direction): Answers "Where is the price going?" (e.g., EMA, VWAP, Moving Average crosses).

Momentum (Force): Answers "How much push is behind the move?" (e.g., RSI, MACD, Stochastic).

Volume (Participation): Answers "Is real money actually backing this move?" (e.g., Volume Profile, OBV).

Volatility (Room to Move): Answers "Is the market giving your trade room to work, or is it compressed?" (e.g., ATR, Bollinger Bands).


The Golden Rule: You should pick one indicator from different boxes to build your setup. Never use two from the same box [04:48].


Building a "Signal Stack"


The video walks through how to properly combine these tools to build a "Signal Stack":


A Two-Indicator Example: Using VWAP (Trend) alongside RSI (Momentum) works well because if one says "go" and the other says "wait," you have a legitimate, mathematical reason to pause [05:14].

Adding Volume for Protection: Sometimes, Trend and Momentum look perfect, but the trade still reverses and hits your stop loss. The video introduces OBV (On Balance Volume) as a critical third tool [07:46]. If the price goes up but OBV stays flat or drops, it means big institutional money isn't participating, and you shouldn't take the trade.

A true signal stack is when 3 indicators (like EMA, RSI, and OBV) all answer their distinct questions with a "yes" [08:33].


The Location Filter: The Most Important Rule


Even with a perfect Signal Stack, your trade can still fail if you ignore location [09:05].


Open Space vs. Proven Levels: A valid signal is useless if it fires in "open space" (floating halfway between a support and resistance level). The market will often chop around and stop you out before moving.

The Sweet Spot: You should only execute a trade when your Signal Stack aligns with a proven level [09:40]. This means taking trades at prior resistance levels that have flipped to support, or at swing lows that have formed demand zones.


Ultimately, the video emphasizes that a strong signal generated at the right, pre-tested price level is exponentially more valuable than three perfect signals occurring in the middle of nowhere [11:34]. Location stops you from placing high-probability signals in low-probability zones!

Institutional-spec version — the one worth coding ------------------------------------------------- There is **no single public “hedge fund formula sheet”**. Real shops run venue-specific, asset-specific stacks. But there **is** a public canonical core: market microstructure models for **order flow, impact, toxicity, liquidity, and execution**, plus a separate layer of **technical/state features**. For **spot FX and XAUUSD**, the big constraint is structural: these are largely **OTC and fragmented**, so there is **no single consolidated tape or single consolidated order book** like centralized futures/equities. BIS describes spot FX as decentralized, venue-fragmented, executed across dealer systems, CLOBs, RFQ venues, and single-bank platforms; in recent data, dealer internalisation has been very high in major hubs. LBMA’s published trade-reporting data shows very large OTC gold turnover, but still not a retail-style full public depth feed. For U.S. dark pools/ATSs, FINRA publishes only **delayed** transparency data, not real-time institutional flow. [finra.org+4Bank for International Settlements+4Bank for International Settlements+4](https://www.bis.org/publ/qtrpdf/r\_qt2512.pdf) So the right way to build this for **FOREX / XAUUSD first** is: 1. **Use canonical institutional microstructure models where the market supports them directly**. 2. **Use venue-specific or proxy versions where OTC fragmentation blocks direct observation**. 3. **Treat price-action concepts** like order blocks, Wyckoff phases, FVGs, liquidity sweeps, supply/demand **as formalized state variables**, not as if they were exchange-native primitives. 4. **Fuse everything into one normalized state vector** for alpha, execution, and risk. That is the actual professional architecture. FX microstructure research explicitly centers order flow, heterogeneity, private information, and liquidity as the key short-run drivers. [Norges Bank+1](https://www.norges-bank.no/en/Published/Papers/Working-Papers/2013/WP-201312/) * * * 1) Base market variables ------------------------ Let: bt\=best bid,at\=best ask,st\=at−bt,mt\=at+bt2b\_t = \\text{best bid},\\quad a\_t=\\text{best ask},\\quad s\_t=a\_t-b\_t,\\quad m\_t=\\frac{a\_t+b\_t}{2}bt​\=best bid,at​\=best ask,st​\=at​−bt​,mt​\=2at​+bt​​ Top-of-book sizes: qtb\=best bid size,qta\=best ask sizeq^b\_t=\\text{best bid size},\\qquad q^a\_t=\\text{best ask size}qtb​\=best bid size,qta​\=best ask size Microprice: μt\=at qtb+bt qtaqtb+qta\\mu\_t=\\frac{a\_t\\,q^b\_t+b\_t\\,q^a\_t}{q^b\_t+q^a\_t}μt​\=qtb​+qta​at​qtb​+bt​qta​​ Relative spread: strel\=at−btmts\_t^{rel}=\\frac{a\_t-b\_t}{m\_t}strel​\=mt​at​−bt​​ For **centralized markets**, these are direct. For **spot FX/XAUUSD**, compute them **per venue** and then aggregate across venues vvv with reliability/liquidity weights wvw\_vwv​: mtagg\=∑vwvmt,v,stagg\=∑vwvst,v,∑vwv\=1m\_t^{agg}=\\sum\_v w\_v m\_{t,v},\\qquad s\_t^{agg}=\\sum\_v w\_v s\_{t,v},\\qquad \\sum\_v w\_v=1mtagg​\=v∑​wv​mt,v​,stagg​\=v∑​wv​st,v​,v∑​wv​\=1 That venue-weighted approach is mandatory in OTC/fragmented markets because the venue mix itself carries information. [Bank for International Settlements+2Bank for International Settlements+2](https://www.bis.org/publ/qtrpdf/r\_qt2512.pdf) * * * 2) Signed trade flow / CVD -------------------------- For each trade iii, define aggressor sign: ϵi∈{−1,+1}\\epsilon\_i \\in \\{-1,+1\\}ϵi​∈{−1,+1} and signed volume: fi\=ϵivif\_i=\\epsilon\_i v\_ifi​\=ϵi​vi​ Then: SignedFlow\[t−h,t\]\=∑i∈\[t−h,t\]ϵivi\\text{SignedFlow}\_{\[t-h,t\]}=\\sum\_{i \\in \[t-h,t\]} \\epsilon\_i v\_iSignedFlow\[t−h,t\]​\=i∈\[t−h,t\]∑​ϵi​vi​ CVDt\=CVDt−1+∑i∈(t−Δ,t\]ϵivi\\text{CVD}\_t=\\text{CVD}\_{t-1}+\\sum\_{i \\in (t-\\Delta,t\]} \\epsilon\_i v\_iCVDt​\=CVDt−1​+i∈(t−Δ,t\]∑​ϵi​vi​ If trade direction is not given, infer it using quote/tick classification; this is standard in microstructure work, though no classifier is perfect. For OTC FX/XAUUSD, use the **best available aggressor tags** from your venue or prime broker; otherwise fall back to **tick-direction**, **quote-move inference**, or **dealer-side flow flags**. The point is simple: CVD is only as good as the trade-sign classification feeding it. [EconPapers+2ScienceDirect+2](https://econpapers.repec.org/RePEc%3Acup%3Ajfinqa%3Av%3A35%3Ay%3A2000%3Ai%3A04%3Ap%3A529-551\_00) * * * 3) Book imbalance (OBI) ----------------------- Top-of-book imbalance: OBIt(1)\=qtb−qtaqtb+qtaOBI^{(1)}\_t=\\frac{q^b\_t-q^a\_t}{q^b\_t+q^a\_t}OBIt(1)​\=qtb​+qta​qtb​−qta​​ Multi-level imbalance over KKK levels: OBIt(K)\=∑k\=1KwkBk,t−∑k\=1KwkAk,t∑k\=1KwkBk,t+∑k\=1KwkAk,tOBI^{(K)}\_t= \\frac{\\sum\_{k=1}^{K} w\_k B\_{k,t}-\\sum\_{k=1}^{K} w\_k A\_{k,t}} {\\sum\_{k=1}^{K} w\_k B\_{k,t}+\\sum\_{k=1}^{K} w\_k A\_{k,t}}OBIt(K)​\=∑k\=1K​wk​Bk,t​+∑k\=1K​wk​Ak,t​∑k\=1K​wk​Bk,t​−∑k\=1K​wk​Ak,t​​ where Bk,tB\_{k,t}Bk,t​ and Ak,tA\_{k,t}Ak,t​ are displayed bid/ask depth at level kkk, and wkw\_kwk​ usually decays with distance from the top of book, e.g. wk\=e−α(k−1)w\_k=e^{-\\alpha(k-1)}wk​\=e−α(k−1) For **FX/XAUUSD**, never pretend you have “the” order book unless you actually do. You only have a **venue-local book** or an **aggregator book**. So build: OBItagg\=∑vwv OBIt,v(K)OBI^{agg}\_t=\\sum\_v w\_v\\,OBI\_{t,v}^{(K)}OBItagg​\=v∑​wv​OBIt,v(K)​ and attach a **coverage confidence score** based on how much of tradable liquidity your data actually sees. The professional mistake is not bad math; it is false observability. [Bank for International Settlements+2Bank for International Settlements+2](https://www.bis.org/publ/qtrpdf/r\_qt2512.pdf) * * * 4) Event-based OFI (the actual institutional microstructure primitive) ---------------------------------------------------------------------- Using the Cont–Kukanov–Stoikov event definition, with PnB,PnAP\_n^B,P\_n^APnB​,PnA​ the best bid/ask prices and qnB,qnAq\_n^B,q\_n^AqnB​,qnA​ their sizes: en\=1{PnB≥Pn−1B}qnB−1{PnB≤Pn−1B}qn−1B−1{PnA≤Pn−1A}qnA+1{PnA≥Pn−1A}qn−1Ae\_n = \\mathbf{1}\_{\\{P\_n^B \\ge P\_{n-1}^B\\}}q\_n^B -\\mathbf{1}\_{\\{P\_n^B \\le P\_{n-1}^B\\}}q\_{n-1}^B -\\mathbf{1}\_{\\{P\_n^A \\le P\_{n-1}^A\\}}q\_n^A +\\mathbf{1}\_{\\{P\_n^A \\ge P\_{n-1}^A\\}}q\_{n-1}^Aen​\=1{PnB​≥Pn−1B​}​qnB​−1{PnB​≤Pn−1B​}​qn−1B​−1{PnA​≤Pn−1A​}​qnA​+1{PnA​≥Pn−1A​}​qn−1A​ Aggregate over a window: OFIt\=∑n∈WtenOFI\_t=\\sum\_{n \\in W\_t} e\_nOFIt​\=n∈Wt​∑​en​ Then the short-horizon price-impact model is: Δmt\=βt OFIt+εt\\Delta m\_t = \\beta\_t\\,OFI\_t+\\varepsilon\_tΔmt​\=βt​OFIt​+εt​ Institutional relevance: this is not retail folklore. Cont, Kukanov, and Stoikov show that short-horizon price changes are driven mainly by OFI, with a slope approximately **inverse to market depth**. That is exactly why OFI belongs in the core state vector. [EconPapers+2OUP Academic+2](https://econpapers.repec.org/RePEc%3Aarx%3Apapers%3A1011.6402) * * * 5) Rolling impact coefficient ----------------------------- You want two rolling impact coefficients, not one. ### 5.1 OFI impact coefficient βt\=arg⁡min⁡β∑u\=t−L+1tωu(Δmu−β OFIu)2\\beta\_t = \\arg\\min\_\\beta \\sum\_{u=t-L+1}^{t} \\omega\_u\\left(\\Delta m\_u-\\beta\\,OFI\_u\\right)^2βt​\=argβmin​u\=t−L+1∑t​ωu​(Δmu​−βOFIu​)2 ### 5.2 Signed-flow / Kyle–Hasbrouck style coefficient λt\=arg⁡min⁡λ∑u\=t−L+1tωu(Δmu−λ Qusign)2\\lambda\_t = \\arg\\min\_\\lambda \\sum\_{u=t-L+1}^{t} \\omega\_u\\left(\\Delta m\_u-\\lambda\\,Q\_u^{sign}\\right)^2λt​\=argλmin​u\=t−L+1∑t​ωu​(Δmu​−λQusign​)2 with: Qusign\=∑i∈uϵiviQ\_u^{sign}=\\sum\_{i \\in u}\\epsilon\_i v\_iQusign​\=i∈u∑​ϵi​vi​ or a concave-size version: Δmu\=λt sign(Qu) ∣Qu∣ψ+ηu,0<ψ≤1\\Delta m\_u=\\lambda\_t \\,\\text{sign}(Q\_u)\\,|Q\_u|^\\psi+\\eta\_u,\\qquad 0<\\psi\\le 1Δmu​\=λt​sign(Qu​)∣Qu​∣ψ+ηu​,0<ψ≤1 Interpretation: βt\\beta\_tβt​ tells you how sensitive the book is to **queue imbalance**; λt\\lambda\_tλt​ tells you how sensitive the price is to **signed executed flow**. Hasbrouck’s framework is about the ultimate price impact of trade innovations; Kyle’s λ\\lambdaλ is the classic liquidity/impact parameter. In live systems, both should be estimated rolling, robustly, and by session. [EconPapers+2Econometric Society+2](https://econpapers.repec.org/RePEc%3Abla%3Ajfinan%3Av%3A46%3Ay%3A1991%3Ai%3A1%3Ap%3A179-207) * * * 6) Toxicity filter (VPIN-style) ------------------------------- Set a fixed bucket size V\\\*V^\\\*V\\\*. For each volume bucket bbb: Vb+\=buy volume in bucket b,Vb−\=sell volume in bucket bV\_b^+ = \\text{buy volume in bucket } b,\\qquad V\_b^- = \\text{sell volume in bucket } bVb+​\=buy volume in bucket b,Vb−​\=sell volume in bucket b Volume imbalance per bucket: Ib\=∣Vb+−Vb−∣V\\\*I\_b=\\frac{|V\_b^+ - V\_b^-|}{V^\\\*}Ib​\=V\\\*∣Vb+​−Vb−​∣​ VPIN over the last NNN buckets: VPINt\=1N∑b\=t−N+1tIbVPIN\_t=\\frac{1}{N}\\sum\_{b=t-N+1}^{t} I\_bVPINt​\=N1​b\=t−N+1∑t​Ib​ This is a **toxicity / adverse-selection** feature, not an oracle. Easley, López de Prado, and O’Hara propose VPIN as a volume-time toxicity measure. Later work challenged its robustness and argued that some predictive power is mechanical or classification-driven. The sane institutional use is therefore: **keep VPIN-style toxicity as one filter among many**, and always pair it with better trade classification and explicit spread/depth/resiliency checks. [pure.au.dk+3IDEAS/RePEc+3OUP Academic+3](https://ideas.repec.org/a/oup/rfinst/v25y2012i5p1457-1493.html) * * * 7) Absorption / replenishment / resiliency ------------------------------------------ This is where people usually get sloppy. Don’t. ### 7.1 Absorption Measure how much aggressive volume is absorbed with little price displacement: Abst+\=VWtaggr,+1+∣ΔmWt∣/δp,Abst−\=VWtaggr,−1+∣ΔmWt∣/δpAbs\_t^{+}=\\frac{V^{aggr,+}\_{W\_t}}{1+\\left|\\Delta m\_{W\_t}\\right|/\\delta p} ,\\qquad Abs\_t^{-}=\\frac{V^{aggr,-}\_{W\_t}}{1+\\left|\\Delta m\_{W\_t}\\right|/\\delta p}Abst+​\=1+∣ΔmWt​​∣/δpVWt​aggr,+​​,Abst−​\=1+∣ΔmWt​​∣/δpVWt​aggr,−​​ Large same-side aggressive volume with small move = strong absorption. ### 7.2 Replenishment After a liquidity shock, measure how much displayed depth refills: Repltb\=Dpostb−DminbVshocksell+ϵ,Replta\=Dposta−DminaVshockbuy+ϵRepl\_t^{b}=\\frac{D^{b}\_{post}-D^{b}\_{min}}{V^{sell}\_{shock}+\\epsilon} ,\\qquad Repl\_t^{a}=\\frac{D^{a}\_{post}-D^{a}\_{min}}{V^{buy}\_{shock}+\\epsilon}Repltb​\=Vshocksell​+ϵDpostb​−Dminb​​,Replta​\=Vshockbuy​+ϵDposta​−Dmina​​ ### 7.3 Resiliency Let τs\\tau\_sτs​ be the time for spread to revert to baseline and τd\\tau\_dτd​ the time for depth to revert. Then a compact resiliency score is: Resilt\=exp⁡ ⁣(−τsτˉs)exp⁡ ⁣(−τdτˉd)Resil\_t=\\exp\\!\\left(-\\frac{\\tau\_s}{\\bar\\tau\_s}\\right)\\exp\\!\\left(-\\frac{\\tau\_d}{\\bar\\tau\_d}\\right)Resilt​\=exp(−τˉs​τs​​)exp(−τˉd​τd​​) or, if you estimate impulse responses directly, use half-lives from the spread/depth/intensity response. The literature on order-book resiliency is explicit: liquidity shocks widen/spoil liquidity immediately, then replenishment speed matters enormously. [ScienceDirect+1](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0165188915001797) * * * 8) Metaorder impact for execution --------------------------------- For a parent order of size QQQ, daily volume VDV\_DVD​, and volatility σD\\sigma\_DσD​, the public institutional benchmark is the **square-root impact law**: I(Q)≈Y σDQVDI(Q)\\approx Y\\,\\sigma\_D \\sqrt{\\frac{Q}{V\_D}}I(Q)≈YσD​VD​Q​​ A participation-aware version is: I(Q,γ)≈Y σDQVD f(γ)I(Q,\\gamma)\\approx Y\\,\\sigma\_D \\sqrt{\\frac{Q}{V\_D}}\\,f(\\gamma)I(Q,γ)≈YσD​VD​Q​​f(γ) where γ\\gammaγ is participation rate. Recent work continues to support square-root scaling at the metaorder level, with participation effects layered on top. This is execution math, not social-media incense. [Taylor & Francis Online+2EconPapers+2](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/14697688.2013.848464) For explicit scheduling, use an Almgren–Chriss-style objective: min⁡{nk}E\[C\]+Λ Var(C)\\min\_{\\{n\_k\\}} \\mathbb{E}\[C\]+\\Lambda\\,\\mathrm{Var}(C){nk​}min​E\[C\]+ΛVar(C) subject to: Xk\=Xk−1−nk,X0\=Q,XN\=0X\_k=X\_{k-1}-n\_k,\\qquad X\_0=Q,\\qquad X\_N=0Xk​\=Xk−1​−nk​,X0​\=Q,XN​\=0 with price dynamics: Sk\=Sk−1+στ ξk+γnkS\_k=S\_{k-1}+\\sigma \\sqrt{\\tau}\\,\\xi\_k+\\gamma n\_kSk​\=Sk−1​+στ​ξk​+γnk​ execution price: Sk\=Sk+ηnkτ\\tilde S\_k=S\_k+\\eta \\frac{n\_k}{\\tau}Sk​\=Sk​+ητnk​​ and cost: C\=∑k\=1NnkSk−QS0C=\\sum\_{k=1}^{N} n\_k \\tilde S\_k - Q S\_0C\=k\=1∑N​nk​Sk​−QS0​ That gives you the implementation-shortfall backbone. [Econometric Society+2Mathematical Institute, Oxford+2](https://www.econometricsociety.org/publications/econometrica/1985/11/01/continuous-auctions-and-insider-trading) * * * 9) VWAP, TWAP, POV ------------------ ### VWAP benchmark VWAP\[0,T\]\=∑ipivi∑iviVWAP\_{\[0,T\]}=\\frac{\\sum\_{i} p\_i v\_i}{\\sum\_i v\_i}VWAP\[0,T\]​\=∑i​vi​∑i​pi​vi​​ ### TWAP benchmark TWAP\[0,T\]\=1N∑k\=1NpkTWAP\_{\[0,T\]}=\\frac{1}{N}\\sum\_{k=1}^{N} p\_kTWAP\[0,T\]​\=N1​k\=1∑N​pk​ or in continuous time: TWAP\=1T∫0Tp(t) dtTWAP=\\frac{1}{T}\\int\_0^T p(t)\\,dtTWAP\=T1​∫0T​p(t)dt ### POV schedule With participation rate ρ\\rhoρ: nt\=min⁡(Xrem,t,ρ Vtmkt)n\_t=\\min\\left(X\_{rem,t}, \\rho\\,V^{mkt}\_t\\right)nt​\=min(Xrem,t​,ρVtmkt​) ### Schedule targets If u(t)u(t)u(t) is the expected intraday volume curve: ∫0Tu(t) dt\=1,x′(t)\=Q u(t)\\int\_0^T u(t)\\,dt=1,\\qquad x'(t)=Q\\,u(t)∫0T​u(t)dt\=1,x′(t)\=Qu(t) Kato shows VWAP is optimal for a risk-neutral trader in an Almgren–Chriss setting with a volume process; TWAP is the flat-time benchmark; POV is the “stay a fixed fraction of market flow” schedule. In practice, serious execution stacks switch among these depending on urgency, toxicity, spread, and liquidity refill. [J-STAGE+2Mathematical Institute, Oxford+2](https://www.jstage.jst.go.jp/article/jsiaml/7/0/7\_33/\_article) * * * 10) Volatility layer -------------------- Use **four** estimators, not one. ### 10.1 Realized variance RVt\=∑i\=1Mrt,i2RV\_t=\\sum\_{i=1}^{M} r\_{t,i}^2RVt​\=i\=1∑M​rt,i2​ ### 10.2 Parkinson range estimator σP,t2\=14ln⁡2⋅1n∑j\=1n(ln⁡HjLj)2\\sigma^2\_{P,t}= \\frac{1}{4\\ln 2}\\cdot \\frac{1}{n}\\sum\_{j=1}^{n}\\left(\\ln\\frac{H\_j}{L\_j}\\right)^2σP,t2​\=4ln21​⋅n1​j\=1∑n​(lnLj​Hj​​)2 ### 10.3 Garman–Klass σGK,t2\=1n∑j\=1n\[12(ln⁡HjLj)2−(2ln⁡2−1)(ln⁡CjOj)2\]\\sigma^2\_{GK,t} = \\frac{1}{n}\\sum\_{j=1}^{n} \\left\[ \\frac{1}{2}\\left(\\ln\\frac{H\_j}{L\_j}\\right)^2 -(2\\ln 2-1)\\left(\\ln\\frac{C\_j}{O\_j}\\right)^2 \\right\]σGK,t2​\=n1​j\=1∑n​\[21​(lnLj​Hj​​)2−(2ln2−1)(lnOj​Cj​​)2\] ### 10.4 Bipower variation BVt\=μ1−2∑i\=2M∣ri∣∣ri−1∣,μ1\=2πBV\_t=\\mu\_1^{-2}\\sum\_{i=2}^{M}|r\_i||r\_{i-1}|, \\qquad \\mu\_1=\\sqrt{\\frac{2}{\\pi}}BVt​\=μ1−2​i\=2∑M​∣ri​∣∣ri−1​∣,μ1​\=π2​​ Use RV−BVRV-BVRV−BV as a jump proxy. Range-based estimators are often more efficient than simple close-to-close measures, while bipower variation is useful for separating continuous variation from jumps. [Cambridge University Press & Assessment+3EconPapers+3revstat.ine.pt+3](https://econpapers.repec.org/RePEc%3Aucp%3Ajnlbus%3Av%3A53%3Ay%3A1980%3Ai%3A1%3Ap%3A61-65) * * * 11) VSA, footprint, volume profile, market profile -------------------------------------------------- These are **not** exchange-native primitives in OTC FX, so formalize them. ### 11.1 VSA quant layer For bar ttt: Spreadt\=Ht−LtSpread\_t = H\_t-L\_tSpreadt​\=Ht​−Lt​ CLVt\=2Ct−Ht−LtHt−Lt+ϵCLV\_t=\\frac{2C\_t-H\_t-L\_t}{H\_t-L\_t+\\epsilon}CLVt​\=Ht​−Lt​+ϵ2Ct​−Ht​−Lt​​ pattern scores such as: * **No demand**: up bar, narrow spread, low VolZVolZVolZ, weak CLVCLVCLV * **Stopping volume**: down bar, extreme VolZVolZVolZ, strong rebound CLVCLVCLV * **Effort vs result**: high ∣VolZ∣|VolZ|∣VolZ∣ with low normalized range or low net progress That is the quant way to make VSA testable. ### 11.2 Footprint At price level ppp within bar ttt: Δp,t\=Vp,t+−Vp,t−\\Delta\_{p,t}=V^+\_{p,t}-V^-\_{p,t}Δp,t​\=Vp,t+​−Vp,t−​ Δt\=∑pΔp,t\\Delta\_t=\\sum\_p \\Delta\_{p,t}Δt​\=p∑​Δp,t​ Ask-side imbalance: Imbp,t+\=Vp,t+Vp−δp,t−+ϵImb^+\_{p,t}=\\frac{V^+\_{p,t}}{V^-\_{p-\\delta p,t}+\\epsilon}Imbp,t+​\=Vp−δp,t−​+ϵVp,t+​​ Bid-side imbalance: Imbp,t−\=Vp,t−Vp+δp,t++ϵImb^-\_{p,t}=\\frac{V^-\_{p,t}}{V^+\_{p+\\delta p,t}+\\epsilon}Imbp,t−​\=Vp+δp,t+​+ϵVp,t−​​ Stacked imbalance = kkk consecutive price levels where imbalance ratio exceeds threshold θ\\thetaθ. ### 11.3 Volume profile For price bins jjj: VPj\=∑i: pi∈binjviVP\_j=\\sum\_{i:\\,p\_i \\in bin\_j} v\_iVPj​\=i:pi​∈binj​∑​vi​ POC\=arg⁡max⁡jVPjPOC=\\arg\\max\_j VP\_jPOC\=argjmax​VPj​ Construct Value Area by expanding outward from POCPOCPOC until cumulative volume reaches target fraction, usually 70%. ### 11.4 Market profile (TPO) TPOj\=∑τ∈session1{priceτ∈binj}TPO\_j=\\sum\_{\\tau \\in session}\\mathbf{1}\_{\\{price\_\\tau \\in bin\_j\\}}TPOj​\=τ∈session∑​1{priceτ​∈binj​}​ Volume Profile gives **where volume traded**; Market A price level becomes a liquidity pool if repeated pivots lie within tolerance band ϵL\\epsilon\_LϵL​. ### 12.2 Sweep / stop run Bullish sweep of lows: Lt<SL−ϵLandCt\>SLL\_t < SL - \\epsilon\_L \\quad\\text{and}\\quad C\_t > SLLt​<SL−ϵL​andCt​\>SL Bearish sweep of highs: Ht\>SH+ϵLandCt<SHH\_t > SH + \\epsilon\_L \\quad\\text{and}\\quad C\_t < SHHt​\>SH+ϵL​andCt​<SH Excursion score: SweepScoret\=w1excursion beyond poolATRt+w2reversal distanceATRt+w3∣ΔCVDt∣+w4∣OFIt∣SweepScore\_t= w\_1\\frac{\\text{excursion beyond pool}}{ATR\_t} +w\_2\\frac{\\text{reversal distance}}{ATR\_t} +w\_3|\\Delta CVD\_t| +w\_4|OFI\_t|SweepScoret​\=w1​ATRt​excursion beyond pool​+w2​ATRt​reversal distance​+w3​∣ΔCVDt​∣+w4​∣OFIt​∣ ### 12.3 Spread shock SpreadShockt\=st−μsσsSpreadShock\_t=\\frac{s\_t-\\mu\_s}{\\sigma\_s}SpreadShockt​\=σs​st​−μs​​ ### 12.4 Slippage Slippage\=signed(side)⋅Pexec−PdecisiontickSlippage = \\text{signed}(side)\\cdot \\frac{P\_{exec}-P\_{decision}}{tick}Slippage\=signed(side)⋅tickPexec​−Pdecision​​ ### 12.5 Depth fade DepthFadet\=1−DtμDDepthFade\_t = 1 - \\frac{D\_t}{\\mu\_D}DepthFadet​\=1−μD​Dt​​ For FX/XAUUSD, spread changes, quote fade, slippage, and venue-to-venue dislocations are often more observable than “real” consolidated stop-flow. So code what the market actually gives you, not what YouTube promised you. [Bank for International Settlements+2Bank for International Settlements+2](https://www.bis.org/publ/qtrpdf/r\_qt2512.pdf) * * * 13) Dark pools -------------- For **U.S. equities**, dark/off-exchange flow can be proxied with FINRA ATS/OTC data, but it is **delayed**, not a real-time execution edge by itself. For **spot FX**, “dark pool” is the wrong mental model much of the time; the better framework is **internalisation**, dealer inventory matching, non-visible bilateral flow, single-dealer platforms, RFQ venues, and fragmented external hedging. For **XAUUSD**, LBMA data gives turnover transparency, but not a full retail-style real-time dark-pool tape. So: * **Equities**: delayed ATS/off-exchange share, venue concentration, off-exchange trend. * **FX/XAUUSD**: internalisation ratios, venue mix, quote fade, spread resilience, external hedge intensity. That is the actual mapping. [LBMA+3finra.org+3Bank for International Settlements+3](https://www.finra.org/filing-reporting/otc-transparency) * * * 14) Supply & demand, order blocks, Wyckoff phases, FVG ------------------------------------------------------ These are **formalized state features**, not canonical exchange formulas. ### 14.1 Supply / demand zone Define a base BBB followed by an impulse III. Recent Institutional-spec version:


Goal:
Create a Codex-ready implementation prompt that computes the full raw signal inventory, groups signals into Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume, and Volatility categories, selects exactly one active signal per category for the alpha state, always appends Market/Institutional Structures to the active state vector, and preserves every formula from the prior spec while adding explicit EMA, ATR, and Bollinger formulas.


Context:
For FOREX and XAUUSD, assume OTC venue fragmentation. DOM, order flow, OBI, and execution observability must be venue-local or venue-aggregated, not fictional global consolidated feeds. FINRA ATS and off-exchange transparency is delayed. Use Cont-Kukanov-Stoikov OFI and depth-impact structure, and use standard TA-Lib-style RSI, MACD, ADX, ATR, StochRSI, and Bollinger definitions. (OUP Academic)


Constraints:
Preserve every formula below exactly.
Add explicit grouping for Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume, and Volatility.
Add explicit EMA, ATR, and Bollinger formulas and category membership.
Compute all raw signals even if only one signal per category is selected.
Do not require every signal in a category to be true at the same time.
Use one of two selection modes:
SelectionMode = "fixed" or SelectionMode = "dynamic"
In fixed mode, the active category signal is chosen from config.
In dynamic mode, score_{j,t} = valid_{j,t} * abs(z(x_{j,t})) * w_j, sel(c,t) = argmax_{j in G_c} score_{j,t}, CatSignal_{c,t} = x_{sel(c,t),t}
If no candidate in category c is valid, CatSignal_{c,t} = 0 and CategoryActive_{c,t} = 0
Keep DOM, Order Flow, OBI, and OFI as distinct raw signals even if only one Institutional candidate is active.
Keep raw VPIN_t, Resil_t, SpreadShock_t, Slippage_t, DepthFade_t, and InternalisationProxy_t available for hard risk and execution vetoes even if they are not the selected Institutional signal.
Base market variables are always computed and always available.
Market/Institutional Structures are always computed and always appended to the active state vector.
Correlation and dark-pool/internalisation proxies must be computed and stored; they may be appended to the context vector or used as optional auxiliary features by config.
Use z(x_t) = (x_t - mean(x)) / (stdev(x) + eps) for normalization.
Use venue-aware aggregation for FX/XAUUSD and direct centralized-book logic for futures, equities, and crypto when available.
Do not omit, merge, or rename any formula-defined feature.


Formula assumptions:
Use OFI and depth-impact structure consistent with Cont-Kukanov-Stoikov order-book event modeling, with OFI linearly linked to short-horizon price changes and the slope inversely related to depth. Use standard TA-Lib-style RSI, MACD, ADX, ATR, StochRSI, and Bollinger definitions. FX turnover and XAUUSD/gold observability are OTC and fragmented, so venue aggregation is required where direct consolidated data does not exist. FINRA ATS and OTC transparency is delayed rather than real-time. (OUP Academic)


Required output:
Build three layers:


RawSignalBank_t

CategorySelectedVector_t

FinalIntegratedStateVector_t


RawSignalBank_t must compute every formula below.


Base market variables
bid_t = best bid
ask_t = best ask
spread_t = ask_t - bid_t
mid_t = (ask_t + bid_t) / 2
relSpread_t = (ask_t - bid_t) / (mid_t + eps)
bidSize_t = best bid size
askSize_t = best ask size
micro_t = (ask_t * bidSize_t + bid_t * askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t + eps)


If venue aggregated:
midAgg_t = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * mid_{t,v}
spreadAgg_t = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * spread_{t,v}
sum_{v=1..V} w_v = 1


Institutional category signal bank
Institutional candidates are computed separately and only one becomes the active Institutional category signal in CategorySelectedVector_t. All raw Institutional signals remain available in RawSignalBank_t.


2.1 Order Books and explicit Depth of Market
DOMBid_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t}
DOMAsk_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}
DOMTot_t^(K) = DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K)
DOMSkew_t^(K) = (DOMBid_t^(K) - DOMAsk_t^(K)) / (DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K) + eps)
DOMPressure_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * (B_{k,t} - A_{k,t})
Use w_k = exp(-alphaDepth * (k - 1)) or another monotone decay.


If OTC venue aggregated:
DOMBidAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMBid_{t,v}^(K)
DOMAskAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMAsk_{t,v}^(K)
DOMSkewAgg_t^(K) = (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) - DOMAskAgg_t^(K)) / (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) + DOMAskAgg_t^(K) + eps)


2.2 Explicit Order Flow
eps_i in {-1, +1}
flow_i = eps_i * vol_i
BuyFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = +1) * vol_i
SellFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = -1) * vol_i
NetFlow_[t-h,t] = BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]
FlowImb_[t-h,t] = (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]) / (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] + SellFlow_[t-h,t] + eps)
SignedFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} eps_i * vol_i
CVD_t = CVD_{t-1} + sum_{i in (t-dt,t]} eps_i * vol_i


2.3 Order Book Imbalance, explicit OBI
OBI_t^(1) = (bidSize_t - askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t + eps)
OBI_t^(K) = (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} - sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}) / (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} + sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t} + eps)


If OTC venue aggregated:
OBIAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * OBI_{t,v}^(K)


Keep the alias explicit:
OBI_t^(1) = DOMSkew_t^(1)


2.4 Event-based OFI
e_n = I(P_n^B >= P_{n-1}^B) * q_n^B - I(P_n^B <= P_{n-1}^B) * q_{n-1}^B - I(P_n^A <= P_{n-1}^A) * q_n^A + I(P_n^A >= P_{n-1}^A) * q_{n-1}^A
OFI_t = sum_{n in W_t} e_n
DeltaMid_t = beta_t * OFI_t + err_t


2.5 Rolling impact coefficients
beta_t = argmin_beta sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - beta * OFI_u)^2
Qsign_u = sum_{i in u} eps_i * vol_i
lambda_t = argmin_lambda sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - lambda * Qsign_u)^2


Alternative concave-size impact form:
DeltaMid_u = lambda_t * sign(Q_u) * abs(Q_u)^psi + eta_u
0 < psi <= 1


2.6 Toxicity filter
For bucket size Vstar:
Vplus_b = buy volume in bucket b
Vminus_b = sell volume in bucket b
ImbBucket_b = abs(Vplus_b - Vminus_b) / Vstar
VPIN_t = (1 / N) * sum_{b=t-N+1..t} ImbBucket_b


2.7 Absorption, replenishment, resiliency
AbsPlus_t = VaggrPlus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
AbsMinus_t = VaggrMinus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
ReplBid_t = (DepthBid_post - DepthBid_min) / (Vsell_shock + eps)
ReplAsk_t = (DepthAsk_post - DepthAsk_min) / (Vbuy_shock + eps)
Resil_t = exp(-tau_spread / tauSpreadBar) * exp(-tau_depth / tauDepthBar)


2.8 Metaorder impact and execution
Impact_t(Q) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / (V_D + eps))
Impact_t(Q, partRate) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / (V_D + eps)) * f(partRate)


Execution schedule:
min_{n_k} E(C) + LambdaRisk * Var(C)
subject to X_k = X_{k-1} - n_k, X_0 = Q, X_N = 0
S_k = S_{k-1} + sigma * sqrt(tau) * xi_k + gamma * n_k
ExecPrice_k = S_k + eta * (n_k / tau)
C = sum_{k=1..N} n_k * ExecPrice_k - Q * S_0


2.9 VWAP, TWAP, POV
VWAP_[0,T] = sum_i p_i * vol_i / (sum_i vol_i + eps)
TWAP_[0,T] = (1 / N) * sum_{k=1..N} p_k
ContinuousTWAP_[0,T] = (1 / T) * integral_0^T p(t) dt
n_t = min(Xrem_t, rho * Vmarket_t)


Expected volume curve:
integral_0^T u(t) dt = 1
x'(t) = Q * u(t)


Cumulative POV target:
POVTargetCum_t = rho * CumMarketVol_t
POVGap_t = CumExec_t - POVTargetCum_t


2.10 Dark pools and internalisation proxies
For equities:
DPShare_t = OffExchangeVol_t / (TotalVol_t + eps)
ATSShare_t = ATSVol_t / (TotalVol_t + eps)


For fragmented OTC FX/XAUUSD:
VenueShare_{v,t} = Vol_{v,t} / (sum_j Vol_{j,t} + eps)
VenueMixEntropy_t = -sum_v VenueShare_{v,t} * ln(VenueShare_{v,t} + eps)
InternalisationProxy_t = InternalMatchedFlow_t / (TotalObservedFlow_t + eps)


If InternalMatchedFlow_t is unavailable:
InternalisationProxy_t = 1 - ExternalHedgeFlow_t / (TotalClientFlow_t + eps)


QuoteFade_t = CancelledQuotedSize_t / (PostedQuotedSize_t + eps)


Trend category signal bank
Trend candidates are computed separately and only one becomes the active Trend category signal in CategorySelectedVector_t.


3.1 VWAP and TWAP trend distance
VWAPDist_t = mid_t - VWAP_t
TWAPDist_t = mid_t - TWAP_t


3.2 EMAs
alpha_n = 2 / (n + 1)
EMA_n,t = alpha_n * C_t + (1 - alpha_n) * EMA_n,t-1
EMAFast_t = EMA_{nFast,t}
EMASlow_t = EMA_{nSlow,t}
EMASpread_t = EMAFast_t - EMASlow_t
EMASlope_t = EMA_n,t - EMA_n,t-1


3.3 ADX trend strength
PlusDM_t = max(H_t - H_{t-1}, 0) * I(H_t - H_{t-1} > L_{t-1} - L_t)
MinusDM_t = max(L_{t-1} - L_t, 0) * I(L_{t-1} - L_t > H_t - H_{t-1})
TR_t = max(H_t - L_t, abs(H_t - C_{t-1}), abs(L_t - C_{t-1}))
PlusDI_t = 100 * Sm(PlusDM_t) / (Sm(TR_t) + eps)
MinusDI_t = 100 * Sm(MinusDM_t) / (Sm(TR_t) + eps)
DX_t = 100 * abs(PlusDI_t - MinusDI_t) / (PlusDI_t + MinusDI_t + eps)
ADX_t = Sm(DX_t)


3.4 Trendline regression
price_t = a + b * t + err_t
TrendSlope_t = b


Momentum category signal bank
Momentum candidates are computed separately and only one becomes the active Momentum category signal in CategorySelectedVector_t.


4.1 RSI
RS_t = AvgGain_t / (AvgLoss_t + eps)
RSI_t = 100 - 100 / (1 + RS_t)


4.2 MACD
MACD_t = EMA12(C_t) - EMA26(C_t)
Signal_t = EMA9(MACD_t)
Hist_t = MACD_t - Signal_t


4.3 StochRSI
StochRSI_t = 100 * (RSI_t - min(RSI_{t-n+1..t})) / (max(RSI_{t-n+1..t}) - min(RSI_{t-n+1..t}) + eps)


4.4 ROC
ROC_t = 100 * (C_t / (C_{t-n} + eps) - 1)


Volume category signal bank
Volume candidates are computed separately and only one becomes the active Volume category signal in CategorySelectedVector_t.


5.1 VSA
BarSpread_t = H_t - L_t
CLV_t = (2 * C_t - H_t - L_t) / (H_t - L_t + eps)
VolZ_t = (V_t - mean(V)) / (stdev(V) + eps)
ER_t = ((C_t - O_t) / (ATR_t + eps)) * VolZ_t


5.2 Footprint
Delta_{p,t} = Vplus_{p,t} - Vminus_{p,t}
FootprintDelta_t = sum_p Delta_{p,t}
ImbAsk_{p,t} = Vplus_{p,t} / (Vminus_{p-dp,t} + eps)
ImbBid_{p,t} = Vminus_{p,t} / (Vplus_{p+dp,t} + eps)
StackedImbalance_t = 1 if there exist k consecutive price levels such that ImbAsk_{p,t} > thetaImb or ImbBid_{p,t} > thetaImb else 0


5.3 Volume Profile and Market Profile
VP_j = sum_{i : p_i in bin_j} vol_i
POC_t = argmax_j VP_j
Construct Value Area by expanding from POC until cumulative volume reaches the target fraction
POCDist_t = (C_t - POC_t) / (ATR_t + eps)
VAState_t = 1 if C_t > VAH_t, 0 if VAL_t <= C_t <= VAH_t, -1 if C_t < VAL_t


TPO_j = sum_{tau in session} I(price_tau in bin_j)
TPOState_t = 1 if C_t > TPO_VAH_t, 0 if TPO_VAL_t <= C_t <= TPO_VAH_t, -1 if C_t < TPO_VAL_t


Volatility category signal bank
Volatility candidates are computed separately and only one becomes the active Volatility category signal in CategorySelectedVector_t.


6.1 Realized and range-based volatility
RV_t = sum_{i=1..M} r_{t,i}^2
sigmaP_t^2 = (1 / (4 * ln(2))) * (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (ln(H_j / L_j))^2
sigmaP_t = sqrt(max(sigmaP_t^2, 0))
sigmaGK_t^2 = (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (0.5 * (ln(H_j / L_j))^2 - (2 * ln(2) - 1) * (ln(C_j / O_j))^2)
sigmaGK_t = sqrt(max(sigmaGK_t^2, 0))
mu1 = sqrt(2 / pi)
BV_t = mu1^(-2) * sum_{i=2..M} abs(r_i) * abs(r_{i-1})
Jump_t = max(RV_t - BV_t, 0)


6.2 ATR
ATR_t = Sm(TR_t)


6.3 Bollinger Bands
SMA_n,t = (1 / n) * sum_{i=0..n-1} C_{t-i}
Std_n,t = sqrt((1 / n) * sum_{i=0..n-1} (C_{t-i} - SMA_n,t)^2)
BBUpper_t = SMA_n,t + kBB * Std_n,t
BBLower_t = SMA_n,t - kBB * Std_n,t
BBWidth_t = (BBUpper_t - BBLower_t) / (SMA_n,t + eps)
BBPos_t = (C_t - BBLower_t) / (BBUpper_t - BBLower_t + eps)


Market and Institutional Structures
These are always computed and always appended to FinalIntegratedStateVector_t. They are not subject to one-of-N category selection.


7.1 Liquidity, hunts, stops, grabs, sweeps, spread, slippage, depth fade
BullishSweep_t = 1 if L_t < SwingLowPool_t - epsL and C_t > SwingLowPool_t else 0
BearishSweep_t = 1 if H_t > SwingHighPool_t + epsL and C_t < SwingHighPool_t else 0
SweepScore_t = w1 * (ExcursionBeyondPool_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + w2 * (ReversalDistance_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + w3 * abs(DeltaCVD_t) + w4 * abs(OFI_t)
SpreadShock_t = (spread_t - mean(spread)) / (stdev(spread) + eps)
Slippage_t = sign(side_t) * (Pexec_t - Pdecision_t) / tick
DepthFade_t = 1 - Depth_t / (mean(Depth) + eps)


7.2 Supply and Demand zones
RangeBase_t = HighBase_t - LowBase_t
DemandZoneCandidate_t = 1 if RangeBase_t < betaSD * ATR_t and (Close_{t+h} - HighBase_t) / (ATR_t + eps) > kappaSD else 0
SDScore_t = wFresh * Freshness_t + wImpulse * ImpulseStrength_t + wUnfilled * UnfilledFraction_t - wRetest * RetestCount_t


7.3 Order Blocks
BullishOB_t = [Low_{cstar}, High_{cstar}]
OBScore_t = w1 * Displacement_t + w2 * BOS_t + w3 * ImbalanceAdjacency_t + w4 * VolumeConfirm_t - w5 * Mitigation_t


7.4 Wyckoff and market phases
W_t in {accumulation, markup, distribution, markdown, manipulation}
If probabilistic:
WyckoffScore_t = P_t(markup) + P_t(accumulation) - P_t(markdown) - P_t(distribution)
If rule-based:
WyckoffScore_t = mapped signed phase score in [-1, 1]


7.5 Fair Value Gaps
BullishFVG_t = 1 if Low_t > High_{t-2} else 0
BullishFVGZone_t = [High_{t-2}, Low_t]
GapSize_t = Low_t - High_{t-2}
Mit_t = FilledLength_t / (GapSize_t + eps)
UnfilledFrac_t = 1 - Mit_t
FVGScore_t = wGap * (GapSize_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + wOpen * UnfilledFrac_t + wHTF * HTFAlignment_t - wMit * Mit_t


7.6 Pivot levels
PP_t = (H_prev + L_prev + C_prev) / 3
R1_t = 2 * PP_t - L_prev
S1_t = 2 * PP_t - H_prev
R2_t = PP_t + (H_prev - L_prev)
S2_t = PP_t - (H_prev - L_prev)
PivotDist_t = min(abs(C_t - PP_t), abs(C_t - R1_t), abs(C_t - S1_t), abs(C_t - R2_t), abs(C_t - S2_t)) / (ATR_t + eps)


7.7 Objective Support and Resistance
SR_j = cluster centers of swing extrema, profile nodes, and pivots
SRDist_t = min_j abs(C_t - SR_j) / (ATR_t + eps)


7.8 Fibonacci
Fib_r = H - r * (H - L), where r in {0.236, 0.382, 0.5, 0.618, 0.786}
FibDist_t = min_{r in R} abs(C_t - Fib_r) / (ATR_t + eps)


7.9 Trendlines
price_t = a + b * t + err_t
TrendSlope_t = b


7.10 Correlation
rho_xy,t = sum_{i=1..n} (x_i - xbar) * (y_i - ybar) / (sqrt(sum_{i=1..n} (x_i - xbar)^2) * sqrt(sum_{i=1..n} (y_i - ybar)^2) + eps)

Thought for 3m 27s

Goal:
Create a Codex-ready implementation prompt that computes the full raw signal inventory, groups signals into Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume, and Volatility categories, selects exactly one active signal per category, converts selected category signals into a true signal-stack with category pass/fail states, applies a hard location gate from Market/Institutional Structures, and preserves every signal and formula from the previous spec while keeping one signal per category in the active decision layer.


Context:
For FOREX and XAUUSD, assume OTC venue fragmentation. DOM, Order Flow, OBI, OFI, and execution observability must be venue-local or venue-aggregated, not fictional global consolidated feeds. U.S. ATS and off-exchange transparency is delayed. Use Cont-Kukanov-Stoikov style OFI and depth-impact structure. Use standard TA-Lib-style RSI, MACD, ADX, ATR, StochRSI, EMA, and Bollinger definitions. The architecture must avoid indicator echo by selecting one active signal from each category, then combining those selected category signals with always-on Market/Institutional Structures for the FinalIntegratedStateVector_t. The selected category signals must feed a mandatory SignalStackGate_t and a mandatory LocationPass_t before alpha, execution, and risk routing.


Constraints:
Preserve every formula below exactly.
Add explicit grouping for Institutional, Trend, Momentum, Volume, and Volatility.
Add explicit EMA, ATR, and Bollinger formulas and category membership.
Compute all raw signals even if only one signal per category is selected.
Do not require every signal in a category to be true at the same time.
Use one of two selection modes:
SelectionMode = "fixed" or SelectionMode = "dynamic"
In fixed mode, the active category signal is chosen from config.
In dynamic mode, score_{j,t} = valid_{j,t} * abs(z(x_{j,t})) * w_j, sel(c,t) = argmax_{j in G_c} score_{j,t}, CatSignal_{c,t} = x_{sel(c,t),t}
If no candidate in category c is valid, CatSignal_{c,t} = 0 and CategoryActive_{c,t} = 0
Keep DOM, Order Flow, OBI, and OFI as distinct raw signals even if only one Institutional candidate is active.
Keep raw VPIN_t, Resil_t, SpreadShock_t, Slippage_t, DepthFade_t, and InternalisationProxy_t available for hard execution and risk vetoes even if they are not the selected Institutional signal.
Base market variables are always computed and always available.
Market/Institutional Structures are always computed and always appended to the active state vector.
Correlation and dark-pool/internalisation proxies must be computed and stored; they may be appended to an auxiliary context vector or used by config.
Use z(x_t) = (x_t - mean(x)) / (stdev(x) + eps) for normalization.
Use venue-aware aggregation for FX/XAUUSD and direct centralized-book logic for futures, equities, and crypto when available.
Do not omit, merge, or rename any formula-defined feature.
Do not use TrendSlope_t as both a default Trend candidate and a default Structure feature; keep it computed in RawSignalBank_t and use it in StructureVector_t by default.
By default, use anti-echo subfamily selection inside the Institutional category so one Institutional signal is still selected overall, but the category does not become its own echo chamber.
The decision stack must be:
RawSignalBank_t -> CategorySelectedVector_t -> CategoryPassVector_t -> SignalStackGate_t -> LocationPass_t -> FinalIntegratedStateVector_t -> alpha_t, exec_t, risk_t -> TradeGate_t


Formula assumptions:
Use OFI and depth-impact structure consistent with Cont-Kukanov-Stoikov order-book event modeling, with OFI linearly linked to short-horizon price changes and the slope inversely related to depth.
Use standard TA-Lib-style RSI, MACD, ADX, ATR, EMA, StochRSI, and Bollinger definitions.
Use helper identities where needed:
Depth_t = DOMTot_t^(K) or the venue-aggregated equivalent
DeltaCVD_t = CVD_t - CVD_{t-1}
VWAP_t = VWAP_[sessionStart,t]
TWAP_t = TWAP_[sessionStart,t]
For Direction mapping:
Dir_t in {-1, +1}
For signed continuous signals, DirMap(x_t) = sign(x_t)
For RSI, DirMap(RSI_t) = 1 if RSI_t > 50 + bandRSI, -1 if RSI_t < 50 - bandRSI, else 0
For StochRSI, DirMap(StochRSI_t) = 1 if StochRSI_t > 50 + bandStoch, -1 if StochRSI_t < 50 - bandStoch, else 0
For ADX, DirMap(ADX_t) = sign(PlusDI_t - MinusDI_t) if ADX_t >= thADX, else 0
For VAState_t and TPOState_t, DirMap(x_t) = x_t
For volatility candidates, use RegimeMap(x_t) rather than directional sign:
RegimeMap(ATR_t) = 1 if ATR_t in [thATRMin, thATRMax], else 0
RegimeMap(BBWidth_t) = 1 if BBWidth_t in [thBBWidthMin, thBBWidthMax], else 0
RegimeMap(RV_t) = 1 if RV_t in [thRVMin, thRVMax], else 0
RegimeMap(BV_t) = 1 if BV_t in [thBVMin, thBVMax], else 0
RegimeMap(Jump_t) = 1 if Jump_t <= thJumpMax, else 0
RegimeMap(sigmaP_t) = 1 if sigmaP_t in [thSigmaPMin, thSigmaPMax], else 0
RegimeMap(sigmaGK_t) = 1 if sigmaGK_t in [thSigmaGKMin, thSigmaGKMax], else 0
RegimeMap(BBPos_t) = 1 if BBWidth_t in [thBBWidthMin, thBBWidthMax], else 0

Build seven layers:

Base market variables
bid_t = best bid
ask_t = best ask
spread_t = ask_t - bid_t
mid_t = (ask_t + bid_t) / 2
relSpread_t = (ask_t - bid_t) / (mid_t + eps)
bidSize_t = best bid size
askSize_t = best ask size
micro_t = (ask_t * bidSize_t + bid_t * askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t + eps)


If venue aggregated:
midAgg_t = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * mid_{t,v}
spreadAgg_t = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * spread_{t,v}

Institutional raw signal bank
Institutional raw signals are computed separately. Only one becomes InstSel_t in CategorySelectedVector_t. All raw Institutional signals remain available in RawSignalBank_t.


2.1 Order Books and explicit Depth of Market
DOMBid_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t}
DOMAsk_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}
DOMTot_t^(K) = DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K)
DOMSkew_t^(K) = (DOMBid_t^(K) - DOMAsk_t^(K)) / (DOMBid_t^(K) + DOMAsk_t^(K) + eps)
DOMPressure_t^(K) = sum_{k=1..K} w_k * (B_{k,t} - A_{k,t})
Use w_k = exp(-alphaDepth * (k - 1)) or another monotone decay.


If OTC venue aggregated:
DOMBidAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMBid_{t,v}^(K)
DOMAskAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * DOMAsk_{t,v}^(K)
DOMSkewAgg_t^(K) = (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) - DOMAskAgg_t^(K)) / (DOMBidAgg_t^(K) + DOMAskAgg_t^(K) + eps)


2.2 Explicit Order Flow
eps_i in {-1, +1}
flow_i = eps_i * vol_i
BuyFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = +1) * vol_i
SellFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} I(eps_i = -1) * vol_i
NetFlow_[t-h,t] = BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]
FlowImb_[t-h,t] = (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] - SellFlow_[t-h,t]) / (BuyFlow_[t-h,t] + SellFlow_[t-h,t] + eps)
SignedFlow_[t-h,t] = sum_{i in [t-h,t]} eps_i * vol_i
CVD_t = CVD_{t-1} + sum_{i in (t-dt,t]} eps_i * vol_i
DeltaCVD_t = CVD_t - CVD_{t-1}


2.3 Order Book Imbalance, explicit OBI
OBI_t^(1) = (bidSize_t - askSize_t) / (bidSize_t + askSize_t + eps)
OBI_t^(K) = (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} - sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t}) / (sum_{k=1..K} w_k * B_{k,t} + sum_{k=1..K} w_k * A_{k,t} + eps)


If OTC venue aggregated:
OBIAgg_t^(K) = sum_{v=1..V} w_v * OBI_{t,v}^(K)


Keep the alias explicit:
OBI_t^(1) = DOMSkew_t^(1)


2.4 Event-based OFI
e_n = I(P_n^B >= P_{n-1}^B) * q_n^B - I(P_n^B <= P_{n-1}^B) * q_{n-1}^B - I(P_n^A <= P_{n-1}^A) * q_n^A + I(P_n^A >= P_{n-1}^A) * q_{n-1}^A
OFI_t = sum_{n in W_t} e_n
DeltaMid_t = beta_t * OFI_t + err_t


2.5 Rolling impact coefficients
beta_t = argmin_beta sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - beta * OFI_u)^2
Qsign_u = sum_{i in u} eps_i * vol_i
lambda_t = argmin_lambda sum_{u=t-L+1..t} omega_u * (DeltaMid_u - lambda * Qsign_u)^2


Alternative concave-size impact form:
DeltaMid_u = lambda_t * sign(Q_u) * abs(Q_u)^psi + eta_u
0 < psi <= 1


2.6 Toxicity filter
For bucket size Vstar:
Vplus_b = buy volume in bucket b
Vminus_b = sell volume in bucket b
ImbBucket_b = abs(Vplus_b - Vminus_b) / Vstar
VPIN_t = (1 / N) * sum_{b=t-N+1..t} ImbBucket_b


2.7 Absorption, replenishment, resiliency
AbsPlus_t = VaggrPlus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
AbsMinus_t = VaggrMinus_{W_t} / (1 + abs(DeltaMid_{W_t}) / dp)
ReplBid_t = (DepthBid_post - DepthBid_min) / (Vsell_shock + eps)
ReplAsk_t = (DepthAsk_post - DepthAsk_min) / (Vbuy_shock + eps)
Resil_t = exp(-tau_spread / tauSpreadBar) * exp(-tau_depth / tauDepthBar)


2.8 Metaorder impact and execution
Impact_t(Q) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / (V_D + eps))
Impact_t(Q, partRate) = Y * sigma_D * sqrt(Q / (V_D + eps)) * f(partRate)


Execution schedule:
min_{n_k} E(C) + LambdaRisk * Var(C)
subject to X_k = X_{k-1} - n_k, X_0 = Q, X_N = 0
S_k = S_{k-1} + sigma * sqrt(tau) * xi_k + gamma * n_k
ExecPrice_k = S_k + eta * (n_k / tau)
C = sum_{k=1..N} n_k * ExecPrice_k - Q * S_0


2.9 VWAP, TWAP, POV
VWAP_[0,T] = sum_i p_i * vol_i / (sum_i vol_i + eps)
TWAP_[0,T] = (1 / N) * sum_{k=1..N} p_k
ContinuousTWAP_[0,T] = (1 / T) * integral_0^T p(t) dt
n_t = min(Xrem_t, rho * Vmarket_t)


Expected volume curve:
integral_0^T u(t) dt = 1
x'(t) = Q * u(t)


Cumulative POV target:
POVTargetCum_t = rho * CumMarketVol_t
POVGap_t = CumExec_t - POVTargetCum_t


2.10 Dark pools and internalisation proxies
For equities:
DPShare_t = OffExchangeVol_t / (TotalVol_t + eps)
ATSShare_t = ATSVol_t / (TotalVol_t + eps)


For fragmented OTC FX/XAUUSD:
VenueShare_{v,t} = Vol_{v,t} / (sum_j Vol_{j,t} + eps)
VenueMixEntropy_t = -sum_v VenueShare_{v,t} * ln(VenueShare_{v,t} + eps)
InternalisationProxy_t = InternalMatchedFlow_t / (TotalObservedFlow_t + eps)


If InternalMatchedFlow_t is unavailable:
InternalisationProxy_t = 1 - ExternalHedgeFlow_t / (TotalClientFlow_t + eps)


QuoteFade_t = CancelledQuotedSize_t / (PostedQuotedSize_t + eps)


Trend raw signal bank
Trend raw signals are computed separately. Only one becomes TrendSel_t in CategorySelectedVector_t.


3.1 VWAP and TWAP trend distance
VWAP_t = VWAP_[sessionStart,t]
TWAP_t = TWAP_[sessionStart,t]
VWAPDist_t = mid_t - VWAP_t
TWAPDist_t = mid_t - TWAP_t


3.2 EMAs
alpha_n = 2 / (n + 1)
EMA_n,t = alpha_n * C_t + (1 - alpha_n) * EMA_n,t-1
EMAFast_t = EMA_{nFast,t}
EMASlow_t = EMA_{nSlow,t}
EMASpread_t = EMAFast_t - EMASlow_t
EMASlope_t = EMA_n,t - EMA_n,t-1


3.3 ADX trend strength
PlusDM_t = max(H_t - H_{t-1}, 0) * I(H_t - H_{t-1} > L_{t-1} - L_t)
MinusDM_t = max(L_{t-1} - L_t, 0) * I(L_{t-1} - L_t > H_t - H_{t-1})
TR_t = max(H_t - L_t, abs(H_t - C_{t-1}), abs(L_t - C_{t-1}))
PlusDI_t = 100 * Sm(PlusDM_t) / (Sm(TR_t) + eps)
MinusDI_t = 100 * Sm(MinusDM_t) / (Sm(TR_t) + eps)
DX_t = 100 * abs(PlusDI_t - MinusDI_t) / (PlusDI_t + MinusDI_t + eps)
ADX_t = Sm(DX_t)


3.4 Trendline regression
price_t = a + b * t + err_t
TrendSlope_t = b


Momentum raw signal bank
Momentum raw signals are computed separately. Only one becomes MomSel_t in CategorySelectedVector_t.


4.1 RSI
RS_t = AvgGain_t / (AvgLoss_t + eps)
RSI_t = 100 - 100 / (1 + RS_t)


4.2 MACD
MACD_t = EMA12(C_t) - EMA26(C_t)
Signal_t = EMA9(MACD_t)
Hist_t = MACD_t - Signal_t


4.3 StochRSI
StochRSI_t = 100 * (RSI_t - min(RSI_{t-n+1..t})) / (max(RSI_{t-n+1..t}) - min(RSI_{t-n+1..t}) + eps)


4.4 ROC
ROC_t = 100 * (C_t / (C_{t-n} + eps) - 1)


Volume raw signal bank
Volume raw signals are computed separately. Only one becomes VolSel_t in CategorySelectedVector_t.


5.1 VSA
BarSpread_t = H_t - L_t
CLV_t = (2 * C_t - H_t - L_t) / (H_t - L_t + eps)
VolZ_t = (V_t - mean(V)) / (stdev(V) + eps)
ER_t = ((C_t - O_t) / (ATR_t + eps)) * VolZ_t


5.2 Footprint
Delta_{p,t} = Vplus_{p,t} - Vminus_{p,t}
FootprintDelta_t = sum_p Delta_{p,t}
ImbAsk_{p,t} = Vplus_{p,t} / (Vminus_{p-dp,t} + eps)
ImbBid_{p,t} = Vminus_{p,t} / (Vplus_{p+dp,t} + eps)
StackedImbalance_t = 1 if there exist k consecutive price levels such that ImbAsk_{p,t} > thetaImb or ImbBid_{p,t} > thetaImb else 0


5.3 Volume Profile and Market Profile
VP_j = sum_{i : p_i in bin_j} vol_i
POC_t = argmax_j VP_j
Construct Value Area by expanding from POC until cumulative volume reaches the target fraction
POCDist_t = (C_t - POC_t) / (ATR_t + eps)
VAState_t = 1 if C_t > VAH_t, 0 if VAL_t <= C_t <= VAH_t, -1 if C_t < VAL_t


TPO_j = sum_{tau in session} I(price_tau in bin_j)
TPOState_t = 1 if C_t > TPO_VAH_t, 0 if TPO_VAL_t <= C_t <= TPO_VAH_t, -1 if C_t < TPO_VAL_t


Volatility raw signal bank
Volatility raw signals are computed separately. Only one becomes VolaSel_t in CategorySelectedVector_t.


6.1 Realized and range-based volatility
RV_t = sum_{i=1..M} r_{t,i}^2
sigmaP_t^2 = (1 / (4 * ln(2))) * (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (ln(H_j / L_j))^2
sigmaP_t = sqrt(max(sigmaP_t^2, 0))
sigmaGK_t^2 = (1 / n) * sum_{j=1..n} (0.5 * (ln(H_j / L_j))^2 - (2 * ln(2) - 1) * (ln(C_j / O_j))^2)
sigmaGK_t = sqrt(max(sigmaGK_t^2, 0))
mu1 = sqrt(2 / pi)
BV_t = mu1^(-2) * sum_{i=2..M} abs(r_i) * abs(r_{i-1})
Jump_t = max(RV_t - BV_t, 0)


6.2 ATR
ATR_t = Sm(TR_t)


6.3 Bollinger Bands
SMA_n,t = (1 / n) * sum_{i=0..n-1} C_{t-i}
Std_n,t = sqrt((1 / n) * sum_{i=0..n-1} (C_{t-i} - SMA_n,t)^2)
BBUpper_t = SMA_n,t + kBB * Std_n,t
BBLower_t = SMA_n,t - kBB * Std_n,t
BBWidth_t = (BBUpper_t - BBLower_t) / (SMA_n,t + eps)
BBPos_t = (C_t - BBLower_t) / (BBUpper_t - BBLower_t + eps)


Market and Institutional Structures
These are always computed and always appended to FinalIntegratedStateVector_t. They are not subject to one-of-N category selection.


7.1 Liquidity, hunts, stops, grabs, sweeps, spread, slippage, depth fade
BullishSweep_t = 1 if L_t < SwingLowPool_t - epsL and C_t > SwingLowPool_t else 0
BearishSweep_t = 1 if H_t > SwingHighPool_t + epsL and C_t < SwingHighPool_t else 0
SweepScore_t = w1 * (ExcursionBeyondPool_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + w2 * (ReversalDistance_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + w3 * abs(DeltaCVD_t) + w4 * abs(OFI_t)
SpreadShock_t = (spread_t - mean(spread)) / (stdev(spread) + eps)
Slippage_t = sign(side_t) * (Pexec_t - Pdecision_t) / tick
Depth_t = DOMTot_t^(K)
DepthFade_t = 1 - Depth_t / (mean(Depth) + eps)


7.2 Supply and Demand zones
RangeBase_t = HighBase_t - LowBase_t
DemandZoneCandidate_t = 1 if RangeBase_t < betaSD * ATR_t and (Close_{t+h} - HighBase_t) / (ATR_t + eps) > kappaSD else 0
SDScore_t = wFresh * Freshness_t + wImpulse * ImpulseStrength_t + wUnfilled * UnfilledFraction_t - wRetest * RetestCount_t


7.3 Order Blocks
BullishOB_t = [Low_{cstar}, High_{cstar}]
OBScore_t = w1 * Displacement_t + w2 * BOS_t + w3 * ImbalanceAdjacency_t + w4 * VolumeConfirm_t - w5 * Mitigation_t


7.4 Wyckoff and market phases
W_t in {accumulation, markup, distribution, markdown, manipulation}
If probabilistic:
WyckoffScore_t = P_t(markup) + P_t(accumulation) - P_t(markdown) - P_t(distribution)
If rule-based:
WyckoffScore_t = mapped signed phase score in [-1, 1]


7.5 Fair Value Gaps
BullishFVG_t = 1 if Low_t > High_{t-2} else 0
BullishFVGZone_t = [High_{t-2}, Low_t]
GapSize_t = Low_t - High_{t-2}
Mit_t = FilledLength_t / (GapSize_t + eps)
UnfilledFrac_t = 1 - Mit_t
FVGScore_t = wGap * (GapSize_t / (ATR_t + eps)) + wOpen * UnfilledFrac_t + wHTF * HTFAlignment_t - wMit * Mit_t


7.6 Pivot levels
PP_t = (H_prev + L_prev + C_prev) / 3
R1_t = 2 * PP_t - L_prev
S1_t = 2 * PP_t - H_prev
R2_t = PP_t + (H_prev - L_prev)
S2_t = PP_t - (H_prev - L_prev)
PivotDist_t = min(abs(C_t - PP_t), abs(C_t - R1_t), abs(C_t - S1_t), abs(C_t - R2_t), abs(C_t - S2_t)) / (ATR_t + eps)


7.7 Objective Support and Resistance
SR_j = cluster centers of swing extrema, profile nodes, and pivots
SRDist_t = min_j abs(C_t - SR_j) / (ATR_t + eps)


7.8 Fibonacci
Fib_r = H - r * (H - L), where r in {0.236, 0.382, 0.5, 0.618, 0.786}
FibDist_t = min_{r in R} abs(C_t - Fib_r) / (ATR_t + eps)


7.9 Trendlines
price_t = a + b * t + err_t
TrendSlope_t = b


7.10 Correlation
rho_xy,t = sum_{i=1..n} (x_i - xbar) * (y_i - ybar) / (sqrt(sum_{i=1..n} (x_i - xbar)^2) * sqrt(sum_{i=1..n} (y_i - ybar)^2) + eps)

8.6 General selection

VolSel_t = CatSignal_{Volume,t}
VolaSel_t = CatSignal_{Volatility,t}


Normalization
z(x_t) = (x_t - mean(x)) / (stdev(x) + eps)

Category pass logic and signal stack
Convert selected category signals into pass/fail confirmation states before alpha, execution, and risk routing.


InstPass_t = 1 if DirMap(InstSel_t) = Dir_t and abs(z(InstSel_t)) >= thInst else 0
TrendPass_t = 1 if DirMap(TrendSel_t) = Dir_t and abs(z(TrendSel_t)) >= thTrend else 0
MomPass_t = 1 if DirMap(MomSel_t) = Dir_t and abs(z(MomSel_t)) >= thMom else 0
VolPass_t = 1 if DirMap(VolSel_t) = Dir_t and abs(z(VolSel_t)) >= thVol else 0
VolaPass_t = 1 if RegimeMap(VolaSel_t) = 1 else 0


SignalStackScore_t = InstPass_t + TrendPass_t + MomPass_t + VolPass_t + VolaPass_t
SignalStackGate_t = 1 if SignalStackScore_t >= MinCategoryVotes else 0


Hard location gate
LocationScore_t = I(PivotDist_t <= thPivot) + I(SRDist_t <= thSR) + I(FibDist_t <= thFib) + I(SDScore_t >= thSD) + I(OBScore_t >= thOB) + I(FVGScore_t >= thFVG) + I(SweepScore_t >= thSweep) + I(Dir_t * WyckoffScore_t >= thWyckoff)
LocationPass_t = 1 if LocationScore_t >= MinLocationVotes else 0
